authors_text: Silvia Cascianelli,Marcella Cornia,Lorenzo Baraldi,Rita Cucchiara
title: Boosting Modern and Historical Handwritten Text Recognition with Deformable Convolutions
title_zh: 利用可变形卷积增强现代和历史手写文本识别
comment: ef:International Journal on Document Analysis and Recognition (IJDAR), 2022, 1-11
comments: ef:International Journal on Document Analysis and Recognition (IJDAR), 2022, 1-11
abstract: Handwritten Text Recognition (HTR) in free-layout pages is a challenging image understanding task that can provide a relevant boost to the digitization of handwritten documents and reuse of their content. The task becomes even more challenging when dealing with historical documents due to the variability of the writing style and degradation of the page quality. State-of-the-art HTR approaches typically couple recurrent structures for sequence modeling with Convolutional Neural Networks for visual feature extraction. Since convolutional kernels are defined on fixed grids and focus on all input pixels independently while moving over the input image, this strategy disregards the fact that handwritten characters can vary in shape, scale, and orientation even within the same document and that the ink pixels are more relevant than the background ones. To cope with these specific HTR difficulties, we propose to adopt deformable convolutions, which can deform depending on the input at hand and better adapt to the geometric variations of the text. We design two deformable architectures and conduct extensive experiments on both modern and historical datasets. Experimental results confirm the suitability of deformable convolutions for the HTR task.
summary: Handwritten Text Recognition (HTR) in free-layout pages is a challenging image understanding task that can provide a relevant boost to the digitization of handwritten documents and reuse of their content. The task becomes even more challenging when dealing with historical documents due to the variability of the writing style and degradation of the page quality. State-of-the-art HTR approaches typically couple recurrent structures for sequence modeling with Convolutional Neural Networks for visual feature extraction. Since convolutional kernels are defined on fixed grids and focus on all input pixels independently while moving over the input image, this strategy disregards the fact that handwritten characters can vary in shape, scale, and orientation even within the same document and that the ink pixels are more relevant than the background ones. To cope with these specific HTR difficulties, we propose to adopt deformable convolutions, which can deform depending on the input at hand and better adapt to the geometric variations of the text. We design two deformable architectures and conduct extensive experiments on both modern and historical datasets. Experimental results confirm the suitability of deformable convolutions for the HTR task.
abstract_zh: 自由布局页面中的手写文本识别 (HTR) 是一项具有挑战性的图像理解任务，可以极大地促进手写文档的数字化及其内容的重用。在处理历史文档时，由于写作风格的变化和页面质量的下降，这项任务变得更加具有挑战性。最先进的 HTR 方法通常将用于序列建模的循环结构与用于视觉特征提取的卷积神经网络结合起来。由于卷积核是在固定网格上定义的，并且在输入图像上移动时独立地关注所有输入像素，因此该策略忽略了这样一个事实：即使在同一文档中，手写字符的形状、比例和方向也可能发生变化，并且墨水像素比背景像素更相关。为了应对这些特定的 HTR 困难，我们建议采用可变形卷积，它可以根据手头的输入进行变形，并更好地适应文本的几何变化。我们设计了两种可变形架构，并对现代和历史数据集进行了广泛的实验。实验结果证实了可变形卷积对于 HTR 任务的适用性。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出使用可变形卷积提升现代和历史手写文本识别的性能，以应对字符形状、尺度和方向变化以及页面退化等挑战。
AI.motivation: 传统卷积核固定网格结构无法适应手写字符的几何变化和墨水像素的重要性，导致历史文档识别效果受限。
AI.method: 设计两种基于可变形卷积的架构，通过动态调整卷积核形状以适应文本的几何变形，并在现代与历史手写数据集上进行验证。
AI.result: 实验表明可变形卷积有效提升了手写文本识别准确率，尤其在历史文档退化场景下表现优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 可变形卷积能有效解决HTR任务中字符几何变化和页面退化问题，为手写文档数字化提供新思路。

The task becomes even more challenging when dealing with historical documents due to the variability of the writing style and degradation of the page quality. Since convolutional kernels are defined on fixed grids and focus on all input pixels independently while moving over the input image, this strategy disregards the fact that handwritten characters can vary in shape, scale, and orientation even within the same document and that the ink pixels are more relevant than the background ones. To cope with these specific HTR difficulties, we propose to adopt deformable convolutions, which can deform depending on the input at hand and better adapt to the geometric variations of the text. authors_text: Jing Jiang,Weihong Deng
title: Disentangling Identity and Pose for Facial Expression Recognition
title_zh: 理清身份和姿势以进行面部表情识别
abstract: Facial expression recognition (FER) is a challenging problem because the expression component is always entangled with other irrelevant factors, such as identity and head pose. In this work, we propose an identity and pose disentangled facial expression recognition (IPD-FER) model to learn more discriminative feature representation. We regard the holistic facial representation as the combination of identity, pose and expression. These three components are encoded with different encoders. For identity encoder, a well pre-trained face recognition model is utilized and fixed during training, which alleviates the restriction on specific expression training data in previous works and makes the disentanglement practicable on in-the-wild datasets. At the same time, the pose and expression encoder are optimized with corresponding labels. Combining identity and pose feature, a neutral face of input individual should be generated by the decoder. When expression feature is added, the input image should be reconstructed. By comparing the difference between synthesized neutral and expressional images of the same individual, the expression component is further disentangled from identity and pose. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of our method on both lab-controlled and in-the-wild databases and we achieve state-of-the-art recognition performance.
summary: Facial expression recognition (FER) is a challenging problem because the expression component is always entangled with other irrelevant factors, such as identity and head pose. In this work, we propose an identity and pose disentangled facial expression recognition (IPD-FER) model to learn more discriminative feature representation. We regard the holistic facial representation as the combination of identity, pose and expression. These three components are encoded with different encoders. For identity encoder, a well pre-trained face recognition model is utilized and fixed during training, which alleviates the restriction on specific expression training data in previous works and makes the disentanglement practicable on in-the-wild datasets. At the same time, the pose and expression encoder are optimized with corresponding labels. Combining identity and pose feature, a neutral face of input individual should be generated by the decoder. When expression feature is added, the input image should be reconstructed. By comparing the difference between synthesized neutral and expressional images of the same individual, the expression component is further disentangled from identity and pose. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of our method on both lab-controlled and in-the-wild databases and we achieve state-of-the-art recognition performance.
abstract_zh: 面部表情识别（FER）是一个具有挑战性的问题，因为表情成分总是与其他不相关的因素纠缠在一起，例如身份和头部姿势。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种身份和姿势分离的面部表情识别（IPD-FER）模型来学习更具辨别力的特征表示。我们将整体面部表征视为身份、姿势和表情的组合。这三个组件使用不同的编码器进行编码。对于身份编码器，在训练过程中利用并固定了一个经过良好预训练的人脸识别模型，这减轻了先前工作中对特定表情训练数据的限制，并使解缠结在野外数据集上可行。同时，姿势和表情编码器用相应的标签进行优化。结合身份和姿势特征，解码器应生成输入个体的中性面部。添加表情特征后，需要重建输入图像。通过比较同一个体的合成中性图像和表情图像之间的差异，表情成分进一步与身份和姿势分离。实验结果验证了我们的方法在实验室控制和野外数据库上的有效性，并且我们实现了最先进的识别性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种身份和姿态解耦的面部表情识别模型（IPD-FER），通过分离身份、姿态和表情因素以提升表情识别的准确性。
AI.motivation: 面部表情识别（FER）因表情信息与身份、头部姿态等因素纠缠而面临挑战，现有方法受限于特定表达数据集，难以在真实场景中应用。
AI.method: 将面部特征分解为身份、姿态和表情三部分，分别通过不同编码器处理。利用预训练身份识别模型固定身份特征，通过姿态和表情编码器优化对应标签，结合解码器生成中性面部并重建输入图像，通过对比合成图像差异进一步分离表情成分。
AI.result: 在实验室控制数据集和真实场景数据集上均验证了方法有效性，达到当时最先进的表情识别性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过解耦身份与姿态因素，本文方法显著提升了表情识别的鲁棒性与准确性，为真实场景下的FER提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种身份和姿态解耦的面部表情识别模型（IPD-FER），通过分离身份、姿态和表情因素以提升表情识别的准确性。
ai_content.motivation: 面部表情识别（FER）因表情信息与身份、头部姿态等因素纠缠而面临挑战，现有方法受限于特定表达数据集，难以在真实场景中应用。
ai_content.method: 将面部特征分解为身份、姿态和表情三部分，分别通过不同编码器处理。利用预训练身份识别模型固定身份特征，通过姿态和表情编码器优化对应标签，结合解码器生成中性面部并重建输入图像，通过对比合成图像差异进一步分离表情成分。
ai_content.result: 在实验室控制数据集和真实场景数据集上均验证了方法有效性，达到当时最先进的表情识别性能。
ai_content.conclusion: 通过解耦身份与姿态因素，本文方法显著提升了表情识别的鲁棒性与准确性，为真实场景下的FER提供了新思路。

search_text: Disentangling Identity and Pose for Facial Expression Recognition 理清身份和姿势以进行面部表情识别 Facial expression recognition (FER) is a challenging problem because the expression component is always entangled with other irrelevant factors, such as identity and head pose. In this work, we propose an identity and pose disentangled facial expression recognition (IPD-FER) model to learn more discriminative feature representation. We regard the holistic facial representation as the combination of identity, pose and expression. These three components are encoded with different encoders. For identity encoder, a well pre-trained face recognition model is utilized and fixed during training, which alleviates the restriction on specific expression training data in previous works and makes the disentanglement practicable on in-the-wild datasets. At the same time, the pose and expression encoder are optimized with corresponding labels. Combining identity and pose feature, a neutral face of input individual should be generated by the decoder. When expression feature is added, the input image should be reconstructed. By comparing the difference between synthesized neutral and expressional images of the same individual, the expression component is further disentangled from identity and pose. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of our method on both lab-controlled and in-the-wild databases and we achieve state-of-the-art recognition performance. 面部表情识别（FER）是一个具有挑战性的问题，因为表情成分总是与其他不相关的因素纠缠在一起，例如身份和头部姿势。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种身份和姿势分离的面部表情识别（IPD-FER）模型来学习更具辨别力的特征表示。我们将整体面部表征视为身份、姿势和表情的组合。这三个组件使用不同的编码器进行编码。对于身份编码器，在训练过程中利用并固定了一个经过良好预训练的人脸识别模型，这减轻了先前工作中对特定表情训练数据的限制，并使解缠结在野外数据集上可行。同时，姿势和表情编码器用相应的标签进行优化。结合身份和姿势特征，解码器应生成输入个体的中性面部。添加表情特征后，需要重建输入图像。通过比较同一个体的合成中性图像和表情图像之间的差异，表情成分进一步与身份和姿势分离。实验结果验证了我们的方法在实验室控制和野外数据库上的有效性，并且我们实现了最先进的识别性能。 本文提出一种身份和姿态解耦的面部表情识别模型（IPD-FER），通过分离身份、姿态和表情因素以提升表情识别的准确性。 Jing Jiang,Weihong Deng cs.CV

authors_text: Feiqi Cao,Soyeon Caren Han,Siqu Long,Changwei Xu,Josiah Poon
title: Understanding Attention for Vision-and-Language Tasks
title_zh: 了解视觉和语言任务的注意力
abstract: Attention mechanism has been used as an important component across Vision-and-Language(VL) tasks in order to bridge the semantic gap between visual and textual features. While attention has been widely used in VL tasks, it has not been examined the capability of different attention alignment calculation in bridging the semantic gap between visual and textual clues. In this research, we conduct a comprehensive analysis on understanding the role of attention alignment by looking into the attention score calculation methods and check how it actually represents the visual region's and textual token's significance for the global assessment. We also analyse the conditions which attention score calculation mechanism would be more (or less) interpretable, and which may impact the model performance on three different VL tasks, including visual question answering, text-to-image generation, text-and-image matching (both sentence and image retrieval). Our analysis is the first of its kind and provides useful insights of the importance of each attention alignment score calculation when applied at the training phase of VL tasks, commonly ignored in attention-based cross modal models, and/or pretrained models. Our code is available at: https://github.com/adlnlp/Attention_VL
summary: Attention mechanism has been used as an important component across Vision-and-Language(VL) tasks in order to bridge the semantic gap between visual and textual features. While attention has been widely used in VL tasks, it has not been examined the capability of different attention alignment calculation in bridging the semantic gap between visual and textual clues. In this research, we conduct a comprehensive analysis on understanding the role of attention alignment by looking into the attention score calculation methods and check how it actually represents the visual region's and textual token's significance for the global assessment. We also analyse the conditions which attention score calculation mechanism would be more (or less) interpretable, and which may impact the model performance on three different VL tasks, including visual question answering, text-to-image generation, text-and-image matching (both sentence and image retrieval). Our analysis is the first of its kind and provides useful insights of the importance of each attention alignment score calculation when applied at the training phase of VL tasks, commonly ignored in attention-based cross modal models, and/or pretrained models. Our code is available at: https://github.com/adlnlp/Attention_VL
abstract_zh: 注意力机制已被用作视觉和语言（VL）任务的重要组成部分，以弥合视觉和文本特征之间的语义差距。虽然注意力已广泛应用于 VL 任务中，但尚未检验不同注意力对齐计算在弥合视觉和文本线索之间的语义差距方面的能力。在本研究中，我们通过研究注意力分数计算方法，对理解注意力对齐的作用进行了全面的分析，并检查它如何实际代表视觉区域和文本标记对全局评估的重要性。我们还分析了注意力分数计算机制更具（或更少）可解释性的条件，以及可能影响模型在三种不同的 VL 任务上的性能，包括视觉问答、文本到图像生成、文本和图像匹配（句子和图像检索）。我们的分析是此类分析中的第一个，并提供了在 VL 任务的训练阶段应用时每个注意力对齐分数计算的重要性的有用见解，这在基于注意力的跨模态模型和/或预训练模型中通常被忽略。我们的代码位于：https://github.com/adlnlp/Attention_VL

AI.tldr: 本文分析了视觉-语言任务中注意力机制的作用，探讨不同注意力对齐计算方法在弥合视觉与文本特征语义鸿沟中的效果。
AI.motivation: 尽管注意力机制在视觉-语言任务中广泛应用，但其不同对齐计算方法的能力尚未被系统研究，尤其在训练阶段的重要性常被忽视。
AI.method: 通过分析注意力得分计算方法，研究其如何表征视觉区域与文本标记的全局重要性，并探讨不同条件下的可解释性及对三个任务（视觉问答、图文生成、图文匹配）的影响。
AI.result: 首次揭示了注意力对齐得分计算在训练阶段的重要性，为跨模态模型和预训练模型提供了关键见解。
AI.conclusion: 本文的分析为优化视觉-语言模型中的注意力机制设计提供了理论支持，强调了对齐计算方法选择对模型性能的关键影响。

authors_text: Shangqing Liu,Yanzhou Li,Xiaofei Xie,Yang Liu
title: CommitBART: A Large Pre-trained Model for GitHub Commits
title_zh: CommitBART：用于 GitHub 提交的大型预训练模型
abstract: GitHub commits, which record the code changes with natural language messages for description, play a critical role for software developers to comprehend the software evolution. To promote the development of the open-source software community, we collect a commit benchmark including over 7.99 million commits across 7 programming languages. Based on this benchmark, we present CommitBART, a large pre-trained encoder-decoder Transformer model for GitHub commits. The model is pre-trained by three categories (i.e., denoising objectives, cross-modal generation and contrastive learning) for six pre-training tasks to learn commit fragment representations. Furthermore, we unify a ``commit intelligence'' framework with one understanding task and three generation tasks for commits. The comprehensive experiments on these tasks demonstrate that CommitBARTsignificantly outperforms previous pre-trained works for code. Further analysis also reveals each pre-training task enhances the model performance.
summary: GitHub commits, which record the code changes with natural language messages for description, play a critical role for software developers to comprehend the software evolution. To promote the development of the open-source software community, we collect a commit benchmark including over 7.99 million commits across 7 programming languages. Based on this benchmark, we present CommitBART, a large pre-trained encoder-decoder Transformer model for GitHub commits. The model is pre-trained by three categories (i.e., denoising objectives, cross-modal generation and contrastive learning) for six pre-training tasks to learn commit fragment representations. Furthermore, we unify a ``commit intelligence'' framework with one understanding task and three generation tasks for commits. The comprehensive experiments on these tasks demonstrate that CommitBARTsignificantly outperforms previous pre-trained works for code. Further analysis also reveals each pre-training task enhances the model performance.
abstract_zh: GitHub 提交用自然语言消息记录代码更改以进行描述，对于软件开发人员理解软件演变发挥着关键作用。为了促进开源软件社区的发展，我们收集了一个提交基准，其中包括跨 7 种编程语言的超过 799 万次提交。基于此基准，我们提出了 CommitBART，这是一个用于 GitHub 提交的大型预训练编码器-解码器 Transformer 模型。该模型通过三个类别（即去噪目标、跨模态生成和对比学习）对六个预训练任务进行预训练，以学习提交片段表示。此外，我们将“提交智能”框架与一个理解任务和三个提交生成任务统一起来。对这些任务的综合实验表明，CommitBART 显着优于之前的代码预训练作品。进一步的分析还表明，每个预训练任务都可以提高模型性能。

AI.tldr: 开发CommitBART模型以理解和生成GitHub提交信息
AI.motivation: 提升对GitHub提交信息的理解与生成能力，促进开源软件发展
AI.method: 基于799万提交的基准数据集，构建CommitBART模型，通过去噪、跨模态生成和对比学习任务进行预训练，并统一提交智能框架
AI.result: 在多个任务中显著优于现有预训练模型，各预训练任务均提升性能
AI.conclusion: CommitBART有效提升了提交信息处理能力，为代码理解与生成提供新方法

authors_text: Hanqing Zhu,Keren Zhu,Jiaqi Gu,Harrison Jin,Ray Chen,Jean Anne Incorvia,David Z. Pan
title: Fuse and Mix: MACAM-Enabled Analog Activation for Energy-Efficient Neural Acceleration
title_zh: 融合和混合：支持 MACAM 的模拟激活，实现节能神经加速
abstract: Analog computing has been recognized as a promising low-power alternative to digital counterparts for neural network acceleration. However, conventional analog computing is mainly in a mixed-signal manner. Tedious analog/digital (A/D) conversion cost significantly limits the overall system's energy efficiency. In this work, we devise an efficient analog activation unit with magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ)-based analog content-addressable memory (MACAM), simultaneously realizing nonlinear activation and A/D conversion in a fused fashion. To compensate for the nascent and therefore currently limited representation capability of MACAM, we propose to mix our analog activation unit with digital activation dataflow. A fully differential framework, SuperMixer, is developed to search for an optimized activation workload assignment, adaptive to various activation energy constraints. The effectiveness of our proposed methods is evaluated on a silicon photonic accelerator. Compared to standard activation implementation, our mixed activation system with the searched assignment can achieve competitive accuracy with $>$60% energy saving on A/D conversion and activation.
summary: Analog computing has been recognized as a promising low-power alternative to digital counterparts for neural network acceleration. However, conventional analog computing is mainly in a mixed-signal manner. Tedious analog/digital (A/D) conversion cost significantly limits the overall system's energy efficiency. In this work, we devise an efficient analog activation unit with magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ)-based analog content-addressable memory (MACAM), simultaneously realizing nonlinear activation and A/D conversion in a fused fashion. To compensate for the nascent and therefore currently limited representation capability of MACAM, we propose to mix our analog activation unit with digital activation dataflow. A fully differential framework, SuperMixer, is developed to search for an optimized activation workload assignment, adaptive to various activation energy constraints. The effectiveness of our proposed methods is evaluated on a silicon photonic accelerator. Compared to standard activation implementation, our mixed activation system with the searched assignment can achieve competitive accuracy with $>$60% energy saving on A/D conversion and activation.
abstract_zh: 模拟计算已被认为是神经网络加速数字计算的一种有前景的低功耗替代方案。然而，传统的模拟计算主要采用混合信号方式。繁琐的模拟/数字 (A/D) 转换成本极大地限制了整个系统的能源效率。在这项工作中，我们设计了一种高效的模拟激活单元，具有基于磁隧道结（MTJ）的模拟内容可寻址存储器（MACAM），以融合的方式同时实现非线性激活和A/D转换。为了弥补 MACAM 的新生和当前有限的表示能力，我们建议将模拟激活单元与数字激活数据流混合。开发了一个全差分框架 SuperMixer，用于搜索优化的激活工作负载分配，适应各种激活能量约束。我们提出的方法的有效性在硅光子加速器上进行了评估。与标准激活实施相比，我们具有搜索分配的混合激活系统可以实现具有竞争力的精度，并且在 A/D 转换和激活方面节省了 60% 以上的能源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于MACAM的模拟激活单元与数字数据流混合的方法，通过融合非线性激活和A/D转换以提升神经网络加速的能效。
AI.motivation: 传统模拟计算依赖混合信号方式，A/D转换的高能耗限制了系统整体能效，需寻找更高效的模拟激活方案。
AI.method: 设计基于磁隧道结（MTJ）的MACAM模拟内容寻址存储器，融合激活与A/D转换功能；结合数字激活数据流并开发SuperMixer框架优化任务分配。
AI.result: 在硅光子加速器上验证，相比标准方案实现超过60%的A/D转换与激活能耗节省，同时保持竞争力的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 混合模拟-数字激活架构有效降低能耗，为高效能神经网络加速提供了可行的硬件实现路径。

The effectiveness of our proposed methods is evaluated on a silicon photonic accelerator. authors_text: Ziyi Ye,Xiaohui Xie,Yiqun Liu,Zhihong Wang,Xuesong Chen,Min Zhang,Shaoping Ma
title: Brain Topography Adaptive Network for Satisfaction Modeling in Interactive Information Access System
title_zh: 交互式信息访问系统中用于满意度建模的脑地形自适应网络
comment: Accepted by Multimedia 2022 (MM'22) as a full paper
comments: Accepted by Multimedia 2022 (MM'22) as a full paper
abstract: With the growth of information on the Web, most users heavily rely on information access systems (e.g., search engines, recommender systems, etc.) in their daily lives. During this procedure, modeling users' satisfaction status plays an essential part in improving their experiences with the systems. In this paper, we aim to explore the benefits of using Electroencephalography (EEG) signals for satisfaction modeling in interactive information access system design. Different from existing EEG classification tasks, the arisen of satisfaction involves multiple brain functions, such as arousal, prototypicality, and appraisals, which are related to different brain topographical areas. Thus modeling user satisfaction raises great challenges to existing solutions. To address this challenge, we propose BTA, a Brain Topography Adaptive network with a multi-centrality encoding module and a spatial attention mechanism module to capture cognitive connectivities in different spatial distances. We explore the effectiveness of BTA for satisfaction modeling in two popular information access scenarios, i.e., search and recommendation. Extensive experiments on two real-world datasets verify the effectiveness of introducing brain topography adaptive strategy in satisfaction modeling. Furthermore, we also conduct search result re-ranking task and video rating prediction task based on the satisfaction inferred from brain signals on search and recommendation scenarios, respectively. Experimental results show that brain signals extracted with BTA help improve the performance of interactive information access systems significantly.

Thus modeling user satisfaction raises great challenges to existing solutions. To address this challenge, we propose BTA, a Brain Topography Adaptive network with a multi-centrality encoding module and a spatial attention mechanism module to capture cognitive connectivities in different spatial distances. We explore the effectiveness of BTA for satisfaction modeling in two popular information access scenarios, i.e., search and recommendation. Experimental results show that brain signals extracted with BTA help improve the performance of interactive information access systems significantly.
abstract_zh: 随着网络信息的增长，大多数用户在日常生活中严重依赖信息访问系统（例如搜索引擎、推荐系统等）。在此过程中，对用户满意度状态进行建模对于改善他们的系统体验至关重要。在本文中，我们旨在探索在交互式信息访问系统设计中使用脑电图（EEG）信号进行满意度建模的好处。与现有的脑电分类任务不同，满意度的产生涉及多种大脑功能，例如唤醒、原型性和评价，这些功能与不同的大脑拓扑区域有关。因此，对用户满意度进行建模对现有解决方案提出了巨大的挑战。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 BTA，一种具有多中心编码模块和空间注意机制模块的脑地形自适应网络，用于捕获不同空间距离的认知连接。我们探讨了 BTA 在两种流行的信息访问场景（即搜索和推荐）中进行满意度建模的有效性。对两个现实世界数据集的大量实验验证了在满意度建模中引入大脑地形自适应策略的有效性。此外，我们还分别根据搜索和推荐场景的大脑信号推断的满意度进行搜索结果重新排序任务和视频评分预测任务。实验结果表明，用 BTA 提取的大脑信号有助于显着提高交互式信息访问系统的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于脑电图（EEG）信号的满意度建模方法，用于改进交互式信息访问系统（如搜索引擎和推荐系统）的用户体验。
AI.motivation: 现有EEG分类任务难以捕捉满意度涉及的多脑区功能（如唤醒度、典型性、评估等），传统方法无法有效建模复杂的脑拓扑特征。
AI.method: 提出BTA网络，包含多中心性编码模块和空间注意力机制模块，通过自适应脑拓扑策略捕捉不同空间距离的认知连接性。
AI.result: 在两个真实数据集上的实验验证了BTA的有效性，基于脑信号的搜索结果重排序和视频评分预测任务性能显著提升。
AI.conclusion: BTA通过建模脑拓扑特征有效提升了交互式信息系统的满意度预测能力，为脑信号驱动的人机交互提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于脑电图（EEG）信号的满意度建模方法，用于改进交互式信息访问系统（如搜索引擎和推荐系统）的用户体验。

authors_text: Jaskirat Singh,Liang Zheng,Cameron Smith,Jose Echevarria
title: Paint2Pix: Interactive Painting based Progressive Image Synthesis and Editing
title_zh: Paint2Pix：基于交互式绘画的渐进式图像合成和编辑
abstract: Controllable image synthesis with user scribbles is a topic of keen interest in the computer vision community. In this paper, for the first time we study the problem of photorealistic image synthesis from incomplete and primitive human paintings. In particular, we propose a novel approach paint2pix, which learns to predict (and adapt) "what a user wants to draw" from rudimentary brushstroke inputs, by learning a mapping from the manifold of incomplete human paintings to their realistic renderings. When used in conjunction with recent works in autonomous painting agents, we show that paint2pix can be used for progressive image synthesis from scratch. During this process, paint2pix allows a novice user to progressively synthesize the desired image output, while requiring just few coarse user scribbles to accurately steer the trajectory of the synthesis process. Furthermore, we find that our approach also forms a surprisingly convenient approach for real image editing, and allows the user to perform a diverse range of custom fine-grained edits through the addition of only a few well-placed brushstrokes. Supplemental video and demo are available at https://1jsingh.github.io/paint2pix
summary: Controllable image synthesis with user scribbles is a topic of keen interest in the computer vision community. In this paper, for the first time we study the problem of photorealistic image synthesis from incomplete and primitive human paintings. In particular, we propose a novel approach paint2pix, which learns to predict (and adapt) "what a user wants to draw" from rudimentary brushstroke inputs, by learning a mapping from the manifold of incomplete human paintings to their realistic renderings. When used in conjunction with recent works in autonomous painting agents, we show that paint2pix can be used for progressive image synthesis from scratch. During this process, paint2pix allows a novice user to progressively synthesize the desired image output, while requiring just few coarse user scribbles to accurately steer the trajectory of the synthesis process. Furthermore, we find that our approach also forms a surprisingly convenient approach for real image editing, and allows the user to perform a diverse range of custom fine-grained edits through the addition of only a few well-placed brushstrokes. Supplemental video and demo are available at https://1jsingh.github.io/paint2pix
abstract_zh: 利用用户涂鸦进行可控图像合成是计算机视觉社区非常感兴趣的话题。在本文中，我们首次研究了不完整和原始的人类绘画的真实感图像合成问题。特别是，我们提出了一种新颖的方法paint2pix，它通过学习从多种不完整的人类绘画到其真实渲染的映射，学习从基本的笔触输入中预测（并适应）“用户想要画什么”。当与自主绘画代理的最新作品结合使用时，我们表明 Paint2pix 可用于从头开始进行渐进式图像合成。在此过程中，paint2pix 允许新手用户逐步合成所需的图像输出，同时只需要一些粗略的用户涂鸦即可准确引导合成过程的轨迹。此外，我们发现我们的方法还形成了一种令人惊讶的方便的真实图像编辑方法，并且允许用户通过仅添加一些适当的笔触来执行各种自定义细粒度编辑。补充视频和演示可在 https://1jsingh.github.io/paint2pix 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出Paint2Pix方法，通过用户交互式绘画实现图像的渐进式合成与编辑。
AI.motivation: 现有可控图像合成方法难以处理不完整或原始用户绘画输入，缺乏对用户意图的准确理解与渐进式生成能力。
AI.method: 提出Paint2Pix模型，学习从不完整人类绘画到真实图像的映射，并结合自主绘画代理实现从零开始的渐进式图像合成与细粒度编辑。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能通过少量用户草图实现高质量图像合成，并支持多样化的精细编辑操作。
AI.conclusion: Paint2Pix为交互式图像生成与编辑提供了有效解决方案，展示了用户意图理解与渐进式生成结合的潜力。

search_text: Paint2Pix: Interactive Painting based Progressive Image Synthesis and Editing Paint2Pix：基于交互式绘画的渐进式图像合成和编辑 Controllable image synthesis with user scribbles is a topic of keen interest in the computer vision community. In this paper, for the first time we study the problem of photorealistic image synthesis from incomplete and primitive human paintings. In particular, we propose a novel approach paint2pix, which learns to predict (and adapt) "what a user wants to draw" from rudimentary brushstroke inputs, by learning a mapping from the manifold of incomplete human paintings to their realistic renderings. When used in conjunction with recent works in autonomous painting agents, we show that paint2pix can be used for progressive image synthesis from scratch. During this process, paint2pix allows a novice user to progressively synthesize the desired image output, while requiring just few coarse user scribbles to accurately steer the trajectory of the synthesis process. Furthermore, we find that our approach also forms a surprisingly convenient approach for real image editing, and allows the user to perform a diverse range of custom fine-grained edits through the addition of only a few well-placed brushstrokes. Supplemental video and demo are available at https://1jsingh.github.io/paint2pix 利用用户涂鸦进行可控图像合成是计算机视觉社区非常感兴趣的话题。在本文中，我们首次研究了不完整和原始的人类绘画的真实感图像合成问题。特别是，我们提出了一种新颖的方法paint2pix，它通过学习从多种不完整的人类绘画到其真实渲染的映射，学习从基本的笔触输入中预测（并适应）“用户想要画什么”。当与自主绘画代理的最新作品结合使用时，我们表明 Paint2pix 可用于从头开始进行渐进式图像合成。在此过程中，paint2pix 允许新手用户逐步合成所需的图像输出，同时只需要一些粗略的用户涂鸦即可准确引导合成过程的轨迹。此外，我们发现我们的方法还形成了一种令人惊讶的方便的真实图像编辑方法，并且允许用户通过仅添加一些适当的笔触来执行各种自定义细粒度编辑。补充视频和演示可在 https://1jsingh.github.io/paint2pix 获取 本文提出Paint2Pix方法，通过用户交互式绘画实现图像的渐进式合成与编辑。 Jaskirat Singh,Liang Zheng,Cameron Smith,Jose Echevarria cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.MM

authors_text: Heng Yao,Sanaz Motamedi,Wayne C. W. Giang,Alexandra Kondyli,Eakta Jain
title: In-vehicle alertness monitoring for older adults
title_zh: 老年人的车内警觉性监测
abstract: Alertness monitoring in the context of driving improves safety and saves lives. Computer vision based alertness monitoring is an active area of research. However, the algorithms and datasets that exist for alertness monitoring are primarily aimed at younger adults (18-50 years old). We present a system for in-vehicle alertness monitoring for older adults. Through a design study, we ascertained the variables and parameters that are suitable for older adults traveling independently in Level 5 vehicles. We implemented a prototype traveler monitoring system and evaluated the alertness detection algorithm on ten older adults (70 years and older). We report on the system design and implementation at a level of detail that is suitable for the beginning researcher or practitioner. Our study suggests that dataset development is the foremost challenge for developing alertness monitoring systems targeted at older adults. This study is the first of its kind for a hitherto under-studied population and has implications for future work on algorithm development and system design through participatory methods.
summary: Alertness monitoring in the context of driving improves safety and saves lives. Computer vision based alertness monitoring is an active area of research. However, the algorithms and datasets that exist for alertness monitoring are primarily aimed at younger adults (18-50 years old). We present a system for in-vehicle alertness monitoring for older adults. Through a design study, we ascertained the variables and parameters that are suitable for older adults traveling independently in Level 5 vehicles. We implemented a prototype traveler monitoring system and evaluated the alertness detection algorithm on ten older adults (70 years and older). We report on the system design and implementation at a level of detail that is suitable for the beginning researcher or practitioner. Our study suggests that dataset development is the foremost challenge for developing alertness monitoring systems targeted at older adults. This study is the first of its kind for a hitherto under-studied population and has implications for future work on algorithm development and system design through participatory methods.
abstract_zh: 驾驶过程中的警觉性监控可提高安全性并挽救生命。基于计算机视觉的警觉性监测是一个活跃的研究领域。然而，用于警觉性监测的算法和数据集主要针对年轻人（18-50 岁）。我们提出了一种针对老年人的车内警觉性监测系统。通过设计研究，我们确定了适合老年人乘坐 5 级车辆独立出行的变量和参数。我们实施了一个原型旅行者监控系统，并对 10 名老年人（70 岁及以上）的警觉性检测算法进行了评估。我们以适合初级研究人员或从业者的详细程度报告系统设计和实施。我们的研究表明，数据集开发是开发针对老年人的警觉性监测系统的首要挑战。这项研究是针对迄今为止尚未得到充分研究的人群的首次研究，对未来通过参与式方法进行算法开发和系统设计的工作具有影响。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对老年人的车内警觉性监测系统。
AI.motivation: 现有的警觉性监测算法和数据集主要针对年轻成年人，缺乏针对老年人的研究，而老年人独立驾驶时的安全性亟需关注。
AI.method: 通过设计研究确定适合老年人的变量和参数，并开发原型系统，对十位70岁及以上的老年人进行了评估。
AI.result: 研究发现，针对老年人开发警觉性监测系统时，数据集的构建是最主要的挑战。
AI.conclusion: 这是首次针对老年人群体的此类研究，为未来通过参与式方法进行算法开发和系统设计提供了启示。

authors_text: Jianyuan Ni,Anne H. H. Ngu,Yan Yan
title: Progressive Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation for Human Action Recognition
title_zh: 用于人类行为识别的渐进式跨模式知识蒸馏
abstract: Wearable sensor-based Human Action Recognition (HAR) has achieved remarkable success recently. However, the accuracy performance of wearable sensor-based HAR is still far behind the ones from the visual modalities-based system (i.e., RGB video, skeleton, and depth). Diverse input modalities can provide complementary cues and thus improve the accuracy performance of HAR, but how to take advantage of multi-modal data on wearable sensor-based HAR has rarely been explored. Currently, wearable devices, i.e., smartwatches, can only capture limited kinds of non-visual modality data. This hinders the multi-modal HAR association as it is unable to simultaneously use both visual and non-visual modality data. Another major challenge lies in how to efficiently utilize multimodal data on wearable devices with their limited computation resources. In this work, we propose a novel Progressive Skeleton-to-sensor Knowledge Distillation (PSKD) model which utilizes only time-series data, i.e., accelerometer data, from a smartwatch for solving the wearable sensor-based HAR problem. Specifically, we construct multiple teacher models using data from both teacher (human skeleton sequence) and student (time-series accelerometer data) modalities. In addition, we propose an effective progressive learning scheme to eliminate the performance gap between teacher and student models. We also designed a novel loss function called Adaptive-Confidence Semantic (ACS), to allow the student model to adaptively select either one of the teacher models or the ground-truth label it needs to mimic. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed PSKD method, we conduct extensive experiments on Berkeley-MHAD, UTD-MHAD, and MMAct datasets. The results confirm that the proposed PSKD method has competitive performance compared to the previous mono sensor-based HAR methods.
summary: Wearable sensor-based Human Action Recognition (HAR) has achieved remarkable success recently. However, the accuracy performance of wearable sensor-based HAR is still far behind the ones from the visual modalities-based system (i.e., RGB video, skeleton, and depth). Diverse input modalities can provide complementary cues and thus improve the accuracy performance of HAR, but how to take advantage of multi-modal data on wearable sensor-based HAR has rarely been explored. Currently, wearable devices, i.e., smartwatches, can only capture limited kinds of non-visual modality data. This hinders the multi-modal HAR association as it is unable to simultaneously use both visual and non-visual modality data. Another major challenge lies in how to efficiently utilize multimodal data on wearable devices with their limited computation resources. In this work, we propose a novel Progressive Skeleton-to-sensor Knowledge Distillation (PSKD) model which utilizes only time-series data, i.e., accelerometer data, from a smartwatch for solving the wearable sensor-based HAR problem. Specifically, we construct multiple teacher models using data from both teacher (human skeleton sequence) and student (time-series accelerometer data) modalities. In addition, we propose an effective progressive learning scheme to eliminate the performance gap between teacher and student models. We also designed a novel loss function called Adaptive-Confidence Semantic (ACS), to allow the student model to adaptively select either one of the teacher models or the ground-truth label it needs to mimic. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed PSKD method, we conduct extensive experiments on Berkeley-MHAD, UTD-MHAD, and MMAct datasets. The results confirm that the proposed PSKD method has competitive performance compared to the previous mono sensor-based HAR methods.
abstract_zh: 基于可穿戴传感器的人体动作识别（HAR）最近取得了巨大的成功。然而，基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 的精度性能仍然远远落后于基于视觉模态的系统（即 RGB 视频、骨架和深度）。不同的输入模态可以提供互补的线索，从而提高 HAR 的准确性性能，但如何利用基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 的多模态数据却很少被探索。目前，可穿戴设备（即智能手表）只能捕获有限种类的非视觉模态数据。这阻碍了多模态 HAR 关联，因为它无法同时使用视觉和非视觉模态数据。另一个主要挑战在于如何在计算资源有限的可穿戴设备上有效地利用多模态数据。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的渐进式骨架到传感器知识蒸馏（PSKD）模型，该模型仅利用智能手表中的时间序列数据（即加速度计数据）来解决基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 问题。具体来说，我们使用来自教师（人体骨骼序列）和学生（时间序列加速度计数据）模式的数据构建多个教师模型。此外，我们提出了一种有效的渐进式学习方案，以消除教师和学生模型之间的表现差距。我们还设计了一种称为自适应置信语义（ACS）的新型损失函数，允许学生模型自适应地选择教师模型或其需要模仿的真实标签。为了证明我们提出的 PSKD 方法的有效性，我们在 Berkeley-MHAD、UTD-MHAD 和 MMAct 数据集上进行了广泛的实验。结果证实，与之前基于单传感器的 HAR 方法相比，所提出的 PSKD 方法具有竞争性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可穿戴设备的跨模态知识蒸馏方法，利用智能手表的加速度计数据进行人体动作识别。
AI.motivation: 可穿戴传感器的人体动作识别（HAR）性能远低于视觉模态系统，且现有设备无法同时利用视觉与非视觉数据，亟需探索多模态数据的有效利用方法。
AI.method: 设计了渐进式骨骼到传感器知识蒸馏（PSKD）模型，构建多教师模型（基于骨骼序列）与学生模型（加速度计数据），提出自适应置信度语义损失函数（ACS）以缩小模型性能差距。
AI.result: 在Berkeley-MHAD、UTD-MHAD和MMAct数据集上的实验表明，PSKD方法性能优于传统单传感器HAR方法。
AI.conclusion: PSKD通过跨模态知识蒸馏有效提升了基于可穿戴设备的HAR性能，为资源受限场景下的多模态学习提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可穿戴设备的跨模态知识蒸馏方法，利用智能手表的加速度计数据进行人体动作识别。
motivation: 可穿戴传感器的人体动作识别（HAR）性能远低于视觉模态系统，且现有设备无法同时利用视觉与非视觉数据，亟需探索多模态数据的有效利用方法。
method: 设计了渐进式骨骼到传感器知识蒸馏（PSKD）模型，构建多教师模型（基于骨骼序列）与学生模型（加速度计数据），提出自适应置信度语义损失函数（ACS）以缩小模型性能差距。
result: 在Berkeley-MHAD、UTD-MHAD和MMAct数据集上的实验表明，PSKD方法性能优于传统单传感器HAR方法。
conclusion: PSKD通过跨模态知识蒸馏有效提升了基于可穿戴设备的HAR性能，为资源受限场景下的多模态学习提供了新思路。
search_text: Progressive Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation for Human Action Recognition 用于人类行为识别的渐进式跨模式知识蒸馏 Wearable sensor-based Human Action Recognition (HAR) has achieved remarkable success recently. However, the accuracy performance of wearable sensor-based HAR is still far behind the ones from the visual modalities-based system (i.e., RGB video, skeleton, and depth). Diverse input modalities can provide complementary cues and thus improve the accuracy performance of HAR, but how to take advantage of multi-modal data on wearable sensor-based HAR has rarely been explored. Currently, wearable devices, i.e., smartwatches, can only capture limited kinds of non-visual modality data. This hinders the multi-modal HAR association as it is unable to simultaneously use both visual and non-visual modality data. Another major challenge lies in how to efficiently utilize multimodal data on wearable devices with their limited computation resources. In this work, we propose a novel Progressive Skeleton-to-sensor Knowledge Distillation (PSKD) model which utilizes only time-series data, i.e., accelerometer data, from a smartwatch for solving the wearable sensor-based HAR problem. Specifically, we construct multiple teacher models using data from both teacher (human skeleton sequence) and student (time-series accelerometer data) modalities. In addition, we propose an effective progressive learning scheme to eliminate the performance gap between teacher and student models. We also designed a novel loss function called Adaptive-Confidence Semantic (ACS), to allow the student model to adaptively select either one of the teacher models or the ground-truth label it needs to mimic. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed PSKD method, we conduct extensive experiments on Berkeley-MHAD, UTD-MHAD, and MMAct datasets. The results confirm that the proposed PSKD method has competitive performance compared to the previous mono sensor-based HAR methods. 基于可穿戴传感器的人体动作识别（HAR）最近取得了巨大的成功。然而，基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 的精度性能仍然远远落后于基于视觉模态的系统（即 RGB 视频、骨架和深度）。不同的输入模态可以提供互补的线索，从而提高 HAR 的准确性性能，但如何利用基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 的多模态数据却很少被探索。目前，可穿戴设备（即智能手表）只能捕获有限种类的非视觉模态数据。这阻碍了多模态 HAR 关联，因为它无法同时使用视觉和非视觉模态数据。另一个主要挑战在于如何在计算资源有限的可穿戴设备上有效地利用多模态数据。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的渐进式骨架到传感器知识蒸馏（PSKD）模型，该模型仅利用智能手表中的时间序列数据（即加速度计数据）来解决基于可穿戴传感器的 HAR 问题。具体来说，我们使用来自教师（人体骨骼序列）和学生（时间序列加速度计数据）模式的数据构建多个教师模型。此外，我们提出了一种有效的渐进式学习方案，以消除教师和学生模型之间的表现差距。我们还设计了一种称为自适应置信语义（ACS）的新型损失函数，允许学生模型自适应地选择教师模型或其需要模仿的真实标签。为了证明我们提出的 PSKD 方法的有效性，我们在 Berkeley-MHAD、UTD-MHAD 和 MMAct 数据集上进行了广泛的实验。结果证实，与之前基于单传感器的 HAR 方法相比，所提出的 PSKD 方法具有竞争性能。 本文提出了一种基于可穿戴设备的跨模态知识蒸馏方法，利用智能手表的加速度计数据进行人体动作识别。 Jianyuan Ni,Anne H. H. Ngu,Yan Yan cs.CV,cs.MM

authors_text: Maryam Soleymani,Mahdi Bonyani,Meghdad Attarzadeh
title: Autonomous Resource Management in Construction Companies Using Deep Reinforcement Learning Based on IoT
title_zh: 基于物联网的深度强化学习在建筑公司的自主资源管理
abstract: Resource allocation is one of the most critical issues in planning construction projects, due to its direct impact on cost, time, and quality. There are usually specific allocation methods for autonomous resource management according to the projects objectives. However, integrated planning and optimization of utilizing resources in an entire construction organization are scarce. The purpose of this study is to present an automatic resource allocation structure for construction companies based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), which can be used in various situations. In this structure, Data Harvesting (DH) gathers resource information from the distributed Internet of Things (IoT) sensor devices all over the companys projects to be employed in the autonomous resource management approach. Then, Coverage Resources Allocation (CRA) is compared to the information obtained from DH in which the Autonomous Resource Management (ARM) determines the project of interest. Likewise, Double Deep Q-Networks (DDQNs) with similar models are trained on two distinct assignment situations based on structured resource information of the company to balance objectives with resource constraints. The suggested technique in this paper can efficiently adjust to large resource management systems by combining portfolio information with adopted individual project information. Also, the effects of important information processing parameters on resource allocation performance are analyzed in detail. Moreover, the results of the generalizability of management approaches are presented, indicating no need for additional training when the variables of situations change.
summary: Resource allocation is one of the most critical issues in planning construction projects, due to its direct impact on cost, time, and quality. There are usually specific allocation methods for autonomous resource management according to the projects objectives. However, integrated planning and optimization of utilizing resources in an entire construction organization are scarce. The purpose of this study is to present an automatic resource allocation structure for construction companies based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), which can be used in various situations. In this structure, Data Harvesting (DH) gathers resource information from the distributed Internet of Things (IoT) sensor devices all over the companys projects to be employed in the autonomous resource management approach. Then, Coverage Resources Allocation (CRA) is compared to the information obtained from DH in which the Autonomous Resource Management (ARM) determines the project of interest. Likewise, Double Deep Q-Networks (DDQNs) with similar models are trained on two distinct assignment situations based on structured resource information of the company to balance objectives with resource constraints. The suggested technique in this paper can efficiently adjust to large resource management systems by combining portfolio information with adopted individual project information. Also, the effects of important information processing parameters on resource allocation performance are analyzed in detail. Moreover, the results of the generalizability of management approaches are presented, indicating no need for additional training when the variables of situations change.
abstract_zh: 资源分配是规划建设项目中最关键的问题之一，因为它直接影响成本、时间和质量。根据项目目标，自主资源管理通常有特定的分配方法。然而，整个施工组织中资源利用的综合规划和优化却很少。本研究的目的是提出一种基于深度强化学习（DRL）的建筑公司自动资源分配结构，可用于各种情况。在此结构中，数据收集 (DH) 从公司项目中的分布式物联网 (IoT) 传感器设备收集资源信息，用于自主资源管理方法。然后，覆盖资源分配 (CRA) 与从 DH 获得的信息进行比较，其中自主资源管理 (ARM) 确定感兴趣的项目。同样，具有相似模型的双深度 Q 网络（DDQN）根据公司的结构化资源信息在两种不同的分配情况下进行训练，以平衡目标与资源约束。本文提出的技术可以通过将组合信息与采用的单个项目信息相结合来有效地适应大型资源管理系统。此外，还详细分析了重要信息处理参数对资源分配性能的影响。此外，还提出了管理方法的普遍性结果，表明当情况变量发生变化时不需要额外的培训。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物联网和深度强化学习的自主资源管理系统，用于解决建筑公司资源分配中的集成规划与优化问题。
AI.motivation: 传统建筑项目资源分配方法缺乏对组织整体资源的集成优化，难以应对复杂多变的项目需求与约束条件。
AI.method: 通过物联网传感器收集分布式资源数据，结合覆盖资源分配（CRA）策略与双深度Q网络（DDQN）算法，训练模型在不同场景下平衡资源约束与目标。
AI.result: 该方法能有效适应大规模资源管理系统，且在环境变量变化时无需额外训练，显著提升资源分配性能。
AI.conclusion: 基于深度强化学习的自主资源管理框架可为建筑行业提供高效、灵活的资源优化解决方案，具有广泛适用性。

authors_text: Zhijun Tu,Xinghao Chen,Pengju Ren,Yunhe Wang
title: AdaBin: Improving Binary Neural Networks with Adaptive Binary Sets
title_zh: AdaBin：使用自适应二进制集改进二元神经网络
abstract: This paper studies the Binary Neural Networks (BNNs) in which weights and activations are both binarized into 1-bit values, thus greatly reducing the memory usage and computational complexity. Since the modern deep neural networks are of sophisticated design with complex architecture for the accuracy reason, the diversity on distributions of weights and activations is very high. Therefore, the conventional sign function cannot be well used for effectively binarizing full-precision values in BNNs. To this end, we present a simple yet effective approach called AdaBin to adaptively obtain the optimal binary sets $\{b_1, b_2\}$ ($b_1, b_2\in \mathbb{R}$) of weights and activations for each layer instead of a fixed set (\textit{i.e.}, $\{-1, +1\}$). In this way, the proposed method can better fit different distributions and increase the representation ability of binarized features. In practice, we use the center position and distance of 1-bit values to define a new binary quantization function. For the weights, we propose an equalization method to align the symmetrical center of binary distribution to real-valued distribution, and minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence of them. Meanwhile, we introduce a gradient-based optimization method to get these two parameters for activations, which are jointly trained in an end-to-end manner. Experimental results on benchmark models and datasets demonstrate that the proposed AdaBin is able to achieve state-of-the-art performance. For instance, we obtain a 66.4% Top-1 accuracy on the ImageNet using ResNet-18 architecture, and a 69.4 mAP on PASCAL VOC using SSD300. The PyTorch code is available at \url{https://github.com/huawei-noah/Efficient-Computing/tree/master/BinaryNetworks/AdaBin} and the MindSpore code is available at \url{https://gitee.com/mindspore/models/tree/master/research/cv/AdaBin}.

Since the modern deep neural networks are of sophisticated design with complex architecture for the accuracy reason, the diversity on distributions of weights and activations is very high. Therefore, the conventional sign function cannot be well used for effectively binarizing full-precision values in BNNs. To this end, we present a simple yet effective approach called AdaBin to adaptively obtain the optimal binary sets $\{b_1, b_2\}$ ($b_1, b_2\in \mathbb{R}$) of weights and activations for each layer instead of a fixed set (\textit{i.e.}, $\{-1, +1\}$). In this way, the proposed method can better fit different distributions and increase the representation ability of binarized features. In practice, we use the center position and distance of 1-bit values to define a new binary quantization function. For the weights, we propose an equalization method to align the symmetrical center of binary distribution to real-valued distribution, and minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence of them. Meanwhile, we introduce a gradient-based optimization method to get these two parameters for activations, which are jointly trained in an end-to-end manner. Experimental results on benchmark models and datasets demonstrate that the proposed AdaBin is able to achieve state-of-the-art performance. For instance, we obtain a 66.4% Top-1 accuracy on the ImageNet using ResNet-18 architecture, and a 69.4 mAP on PASCAL VOC using SSD300. The PyTorch code is available at \url{https://github.com/huawei-noah/Efficient-Computing/tree/master/BinaryNetworks/AdaBin} and the MindSpore code is available at \url{https://gitee.com/mindspore/models/tree/master/research/cv/AdaBin}.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了二元神经网络（BNN），其中权重和激活都被二值化为 1 位值，从而大大降低了内存使用量和计算复杂度。由于现代深度神经网络出于准确性原因而设计复杂，架构复杂，权重和激活分布的多样性非常高。因此，传统的符号函数不能很好地用于有效地二值化 BNN 中的全精度值。为此，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法，称为 AdaBin，以自适应地获取每层权重和激活的最佳二进制集 $\{b_1, b_2\}$ ($b_1, b_2\in \mathbb{R}$)，而不是固定集 (\textit{i.e.}, $\{-1, +1\}$)。这样，所提出的方法可以更好地拟合不同的分布并增加二值化特征的表示能力。在实践中，我们使用1位值的中心位置和距离来定义新的二进制量化函数。对于权重，我们提出了一种均衡方法，将二元分布的对称中心与实值分布对齐，并最小化它们的 Kullback-Leibler 散度。同时，我们引入了一种基于梯度的优化方法来获取这两个激活参数，并以端到端的方式联合训练。基准模型和数据集的实验结果表明，所提出的 AdaBin 能够实现最先进的性能。例如，我们使用 ResNet-18 架构在 ImageNet 上获得了 66.4% 的 Top-1 准确率，在使用 SSD300 的 PASCAL VOC 上获得了 69.4 mAP。 PyTorch 代码可在 \url{https://github.com/huawei-noah/Efficient-Computing/tree/master/BinaryNetworks/AdaBin} 获取，MindSpore 代码可在 \url{https://gitee.com/mindspore/models/tree/master/research/cv/AdaBin} 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出一种自适应二值集方法AdaBin，用于改进二值神经网络中权重和激活值的二值化效果。
AI.motivation: 传统二值神经网络使用固定的符号函数（如{-1,+1}）进行二值化，但现代深度神经网络中权重和激活值的分布多样性强，固定二值集无法有效适配不同分布，限制了模型表达能力。
AI.method: 提出AdaBin方法，为每层网络自适应学习最优的二值集参数{_b1, _b2}。对权重采用均衡方法对齐二值分布与实值分布的对称中心，并最小化KL散度；对激活值采用基于梯度的优化方法端到端学习两个参数。
AI.result: 在ImageNet上使用ResNet-18达到66.4% Top-1准确率，在PASCAL VOC上使用SSD300达到69.4 mAP，达到当时最优性能。
AI.conclusion: AdaBin能有效提升二值神经网络的表示能力和性能，是一种简单且有效的二值化方法。

ai_content_json.method: 提出AdaBin方法，为每层网络自适应学习最优的二值集参数{_b1, _b2}。对权重采用均衡方法对齐二值分布与实值分布的对称中心，并最小化KL散度；对激活值采用基于梯度的优化方法端到端学习两个参数。
ai_content_json.result: 在ImageNet上使用ResNet-18达到66.4% Top-1准确率，在PASCAL VOC上使用SSD300达到69.4 mAP，达到当时最优性能。
ai_content_json.conclusion: AdaBin能有效提升二值神经网络的表示能力和性能，是一种简单且有效的二值化方法。

tldr: 提出一种自适应二值集方法AdaBin，用于改进二值神经网络中权重和激活值的二值化效果。
motivation: 传统二值神经网络使用固定的符号函数（如{-1,+1}）进行二值化，但现代深度神经网络中权重和激活值的分布多样性强，固定二值集无法有效适配不同分布，限制了模型表达能力。
method: 提出AdaBin方法，为每层网络自适应学习最优的二值集参数{_b1, _b2}。对权重采用均衡方法对齐二值分布与实值分布的对称中心，并最小化KL散度；对激活值采用基于梯度的优化方法端到端学习两个参数。
result: 在ImageNet上使用ResNet-18达到66.4% Top-1准确率，在PASCAL VOC上使用SSD300达到69.4 mAP，达到当时最优性能。
conclusion: AdaBin能有效提升二值神经网络的表示能力和性能，是一种简单且有效的二值化方法。
search_text: AdaBin: Improving Binary Neural Networks with Adaptive Binary Sets AdaBin：使用自适应二进制集改进二元神经网络 This paper studies the Binary Neural Networks (BNNs) in which weights and activations are both binarized into 1-bit values, thus greatly reducing the memory usage and computational complexity. Since the modern deep neural networks are of sophisticated design with complex architecture for the accuracy reason, the diversity on distributions of weights and activations is very high. Therefore, the conventional sign function cannot be well used for effectively binarizing full-precision values in BNNs. To this end, we present a simple yet effective approach called AdaBin to adaptively obtain the optimal binary sets $\{b_1, b_2\}$ ($b_1, b_2\in \mathbb{R}$) of weights and activations for each layer instead of a fixed set (\textit{i.e.}, $\{-1, +1\}$). In this way, the proposed method can better fit different distributions and increase the representation ability of binarized features. In practice, we use the center position and distance of 1-bit values to define a new binary quantization function. For the weights, we propose an equalization method to align the symmetrical center of binary distribution to real-valued distribution, and minimize the Kullback-Leibler divergence of them. Meanwhile, we introduce a gradient-based optimization method to get these two parameters for activations, which are jointly trained in an end-to-end manner. Experimental results on benchmark models and datasets demonstrate that the proposed AdaBin is able to achieve state-of-the-art performance. For instance, we obtain a 66.4% Top-1 accuracy on the ImageNet using ResNet-18 architecture, and a 69.4 mAP on PASCAL VOC using SSD300. The PyTorch code is available at \url{https://github.com/huawei-noah/Efficient-Computing/tree/master/BinaryNetworks/AdaBin} and the MindSpore code is available at \url{https://gitee.com/mindspore/models/tree/master/research/cv/AdaBin}. 本文研究了二元神经网络（BNN），其中权重和激活都被二值化为 1 位值，从而大大降低了内存使用量和计算复杂度。由于现代深度神经网络出于准确性原因而设计复杂，架构复杂，权重和激活分布的多样性非常高。因此，传统的符号函数不能很好地用于有效地二值化 BNN 中的全精度值。为此，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法，称为 AdaBin，以自适应地获取每层权重和激活的最佳二进制集 $\{b_1, b_2\}$ ($b_1, b_2\in \mathbb{R}$)，而不是固定集 (\textit{i.e.}, $\{-1, +1\}$)。这样，所提出的方法可以更好地拟合不同的分布并增加二值化特征的表示能力。在实践中，我们使用1位值的中心位置和距离来定义新的二进制量化函数。对于权重，我们提出了一种均衡方法，将二元分布的对称中心与实值分布对齐，并最小化它们的 Kullback-Leibler 散度。同时，我们引入了一种基于梯度的优化方法来获取这两个激活参数，并以端到端的方式联合训练。基准模型和数据集的实验结果表明，所提出的 AdaBin 能够实现最先进的性能。例如，我们使用 ResNet-18 架构在 ImageNet 上获得了 66.4% 的 Top-1 准确率，在使用 SSD300 的 PASCAL VOC 上获得了 69.4 mAP。 PyTorch 代码可在 \url{https://github.com/huawei-noah/Efficient-Computing/tree/master/BinaryNetworks/AdaBin} 获取，MindSpore 代码可在 \url{https://gitee.com/mindspore/models/tree/master/research/cv/AdaBin} 获取。 提出一种自适应二值集方法AdaBin，用于改进二值神经网络中权重和激活值的二值化效果。 Zhijun Tu,Xinghao Chen,Pengju Ren,Yunhe Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Zongyuan Zhang,Qingwen Bu,Tianyang Duan,Zheng Lin,Yuhao Qing,Zihan Fang,Heming Cui,Dong Huang
title: Two Heads are Better than One: Robust Learning Meets Multi-branch Models
title_zh: 两个头脑比一个头脑更好：鲁棒学习遇到多分支模型
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are vulnerable to adversarial examples, in which DNNs are misled to false outputs due to inputs containing imperceptible perturbations. Adversarial training, a reliable and effective method of defense, may significantly reduce the vulnerability of neural networks and becomes the de facto standard for robust learning. While many recent works practice the data-centric philosophy, such as how to generate better adversarial examples or use generative models to produce additional training data, we look back to the models themselves and revisit the adversarial robustness from the perspective of deep feature distribution as an insightful complementarity. In this paper, we propose \textit{Branch Orthogonality adveRsarial Training} (BORT) to obtain state-of-the-art performance with solely the original dataset for adversarial training. To practice our design idea of integrating multiple orthogonal solution spaces, we leverage a simple multi-branch neural network and propose a corresponding loss function, branch-orthogonal loss, to make each solution space of the multi-branch model orthogonal. We evaluate our approach on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 and SVHN against $\ell_{\infty}$ norm-bounded perturbations of size $ε= 8/255$, respectively. Exhaustive experiments are conducted to show that our method goes beyond all state-of-the-art methods without any tricks. Compared to all methods that do not use additional data for training, our models achieve 67.3\% and 41.5\% robust accuracy on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (improving upon the state-of-the-art by +7.23\% and +9.07\%).
summary: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are vulnerable to adversarial examples, in which DNNs are misled to false outputs due to inputs containing imperceptible perturbations. Adversarial training, a reliable and effective method of defense, may significantly reduce the vulnerability of neural networks and becomes the de facto standard for robust learning. While many recent works practice the data-centric philosophy, such as how to generate better adversarial examples or use generative models to produce additional training data, we look back to the models themselves and revisit the adversarial robustness from the perspective of deep feature distribution as an insightful complementarity. In this paper, we propose \textit{Branch Orthogonality adveRsarial Training} (BORT) to obtain state-of-the-art performance with solely the original dataset for adversarial training. To practice our design idea of integrating multiple orthogonal solution spaces, we leverage a simple multi-branch neural network and propose a corresponding loss function, branch-orthogonal loss, to make each solution space of the multi-branch model orthogonal. We evaluate our approach on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100 and SVHN against $\ell_{\infty}$ norm-bounded perturbations of size $ε= 8/255$, respectively. Exhaustive experiments are conducted to show that our method goes beyond all state-of-the-art methods without any tricks. Compared to all methods that do not use additional data for training, our models achieve 67.3\% and 41.5\% robust accuracy on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (improving upon the state-of-the-art by +7.23\% and +9.07\%).
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 很容易受到对抗性示例的影响，其中 DNN 由于输入包含难以察觉的扰动而被误导，得出错误的输出。对抗性训练是一种可靠且有效的防御方法，可以显着降低神经网络的脆弱性，并成为鲁棒学习的事实上的标准。虽然最近的许多工作实践了以数据为中心的哲学，例如如何生成更好的对抗性示例或使用生成模型来生成额外的训练数据，但我们回顾模型本身，并从深层特征分布的角度重新审视对抗性鲁棒性，作为一种富有洞察力的互补性。在本文中，我们提出 \textit{Branch Orthogonality adveRsarial Training} (BORT)，仅使用原始数据集进行对抗训练即可获得最先进的性能。为了实践集成多个正交解空间的设计思想，我们利用简单的多分支神经网络并提出相应的损失函数——分支正交损失，以使多分支模型的每个解空间正交。我们分别针对 $\ell_{\infty}$ 大小 $ε= 8/255$ 的范数有界扰动，评估我们在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 SVHN 上的方法。详尽的实验表明我们的方法超越了所有最先进的方法，没有任何技巧。与所有不使用额外数据进行训练的方法相比，我们的模型在 CIFAR-10 和 CIFAR-100 上实现了 67.3\% 和 41.5\% 的鲁棒精度（比现有技术提高了 +7.23\% 和 +9.07\%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为BORT的方法，通过多分支模型和分支正交损失函数，利用原始数据集提升神经网络对抗样本的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗训练方法多依赖数据增强或生成模型，但本文从模型自身视角出发，通过深度特征分布的互补性提升对抗鲁棒性，避免对额外数据的依赖。
AI.method: 设计多分支神经网络并引入分支正交损失函数，强制各分支的解空间相互正交，从而在原始数据集上实现对抗训练。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10和CIFAR-100数据集上，分别达到67.3%和41.5%的鲁棒准确率，较现有最优方法提升7.23%和9.07%。
AI.conclusion: BORT通过多分支正交解空间设计，在无需额外数据的情况下显著提升了模型对抗鲁棒性，验证了模型结构优化的有效性。

authors_text: Zhengding Luo,Dongyuan Shi,Woon-Seng Gan
title: A Hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS Algorithm for Active Noise Control based on Deep Learning
title_zh: 基于深度学习的主动噪声控制混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法
abstract: The selective fixed-filter active noise control (SFANC) method selecting the best pre-trained control filters for various types of noise can achieve a fast response time. However, it may lead to large steady-state errors due to inaccurate filter selection and the lack of adaptability. In comparison, the filtered-X normalized least-mean-square (FxNLMS) algorithm can obtain lower steady-state errors through adaptive optimization. Nonetheless, its slow convergence has a detrimental effect on dynamic noise attenuation. Therefore, this paper proposes a hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS approach to overcome the adaptive algorithm's slow convergence and provide a better noise reduction level than the SFANC method. A lightweight one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN) is designed to automatically select the most suitable pre-trained control filter for each frame of the primary noise. Meanwhile, the FxNLMS algorithm continues to update the coefficients of the chosen pre-trained control filter at the sampling rate. Owing to the effective combination of the two algorithms, experimental results show that the hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS algorithm can achieve a rapid response time, a low noise reduction error, and a high degree of robustness.
summary: The selective fixed-filter active noise control (SFANC) method selecting the best pre-trained control filters for various types of noise can achieve a fast response time. However, it may lead to large steady-state errors due to inaccurate filter selection and the lack of adaptability. In comparison, the filtered-X normalized least-mean-square (FxNLMS) algorithm can obtain lower steady-state errors through adaptive optimization. Nonetheless, its slow convergence has a detrimental effect on dynamic noise attenuation. Therefore, this paper proposes a hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS approach to overcome the adaptive algorithm's slow convergence and provide a better noise reduction level than the SFANC method. A lightweight one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN) is designed to automatically select the most suitable pre-trained control filter for each frame of the primary noise. Meanwhile, the FxNLMS algorithm continues to update the coefficients of the chosen pre-trained control filter at the sampling rate. Owing to the effective combination of the two algorithms, experimental results show that the hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS algorithm can achieve a rapid response time, a low noise reduction error, and a high degree of robustness.
abstract_zh: 选择性固定滤波器有源噪声控制（SFANC）方法为各种类型的噪声选择最佳的预训练控制滤波器，可以实现快速响应时间。然而，由于滤波器选择不准确和缺乏适应性，可能会导致较大的稳态误差。相比之下，滤波X归一化最小均方（FxNLMS）算法可以通过自适应优化获得较低的稳态误差。尽管如此，其缓慢的收敛对动态噪声衰减有不利影响。因此，本文提出了一种混合SFANC-FxNLMS方法来克服自适应算法收敛速度慢的问题，并提供比SFANC方法更好的降噪水平。轻量级一维卷积神经网络（1D CNN）旨在为每帧主要噪声自动选择最合适的预训练控制滤波器。同时，FxNLMS 算法继续以采样率更新所选预训练控制滤波器的系数。由于两种算法的有效结合，实验结果表明，混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法能够实现快速的响应时间、较低的降噪误差和高度的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合深度学习的混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法，用于主动噪声控制。
AI.motivation: SFANC方法响应快但稳态误差大，FxNLMS算法误差低但收敛慢，需结合两者优势以提升噪声控制效果。
AI.method: 设计轻量级1D CNN自动选择最佳预训练控制滤波器，同时以采样率用FxNLMS算法更新滤波器系数。
AI.result: 实验表明该混合算法实现了快速响应时间、低降噪误差和高鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法有效解决了单一方法的局限性，在噪声控制中表现更优。

method: 设计轻量级1D CNN自动选择最佳预训练控制滤波器，同时以采样率用FxNLMS算法更新滤波器系数。
result: 实验表明该混合算法实现了快速响应时间、低降噪误差和高鲁棒性。
conclusion: 混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法有效解决了单一方法的局限性，在噪声控制中表现更优。
search_text: A Hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS Algorithm for Active Noise Control based on Deep Learning 基于深度学习的主动噪声控制混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法 The selective fixed-filter active noise control (SFANC) method selecting the best pre-trained control filters for various types of noise can achieve a fast response time. However, it may lead to large steady-state errors due to inaccurate filter selection and the lack of adaptability. In comparison, the filtered-X normalized least-mean-square (FxNLMS) algorithm can obtain lower steady-state errors through adaptive optimization. Nonetheless, its slow convergence has a detrimental effect on dynamic noise attenuation. Therefore, this paper proposes a hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS approach to overcome the adaptive algorithm's slow convergence and provide a better noise reduction level than the SFANC method. A lightweight one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D CNN) is designed to automatically select the most suitable pre-trained control filter for each frame of the primary noise. Meanwhile, the FxNLMS algorithm continues to update the coefficients of the chosen pre-trained control filter at the sampling rate. Owing to the effective combination of the two algorithms, experimental results show that the hybrid SFANC-FxNLMS algorithm can achieve a rapid response time, a low noise reduction error, and a high degree of robustness. 选择性固定滤波器有源噪声控制（SFANC）方法为各种类型的噪声选择最佳的预训练控制滤波器，可以实现快速响应时间。然而，由于滤波器选择不准确和缺乏适应性，可能会导致较大的稳态误差。相比之下，滤波X归一化最小均方（FxNLMS）算法可以通过自适应优化获得较低的稳态误差。尽管如此，其缓慢的收敛对动态噪声衰减有不利影响。因此，本文提出了一种混合SFANC-FxNLMS方法来克服自适应算法收敛速度慢的问题，并提供比SFANC方法更好的降噪水平。轻量级一维卷积神经网络（1D CNN）旨在为每帧主要噪声自动选择最合适的预训练控制滤波器。同时，FxNLMS 算法继续以采样率更新所选预训练控制滤波器的系数。由于两种算法的有效结合，实验结果表明，混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法能够实现快速的响应时间、较低的降噪误差和高度的鲁棒性。 本文提出了一种结合深度学习的混合SFANC-FxNLMS算法，用于主动噪声控制。 Zhengding Luo,Dongyuan Shi,Woon-Seng Gan eess.SY,cs.LG,cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Dong Won Lee,Chaitanya Ahuja,Paul Pu Liang,Sanika Natu,Louis-Philippe Morency
title: Multimodal Lecture Presentations Dataset: Understanding Multimodality in Educational Slides
title_zh: 多模态讲座演示数据集：了解教育幻灯片中的多模态
abstract: Lecture slide presentations, a sequence of pages that contain text and figures accompanied by speech, are constructed and presented carefully in order to optimally transfer knowledge to students. Previous studies in multimedia and psychology attribute the effectiveness of lecture presentations to their multimodal nature. As a step toward developing AI to aid in student learning as intelligent teacher assistants, we introduce the Multimodal Lecture Presentations dataset as a large-scale benchmark testing the capabilities of machine learning models in multimodal understanding of educational content. Our dataset contains aligned slides and spoken language, for 180+ hours of video and 9000+ slides, with 10 lecturers from various subjects (e.g., computer science, dentistry, biology). We introduce two research tasks which are designed as stepping stones towards AI agents that can explain (automatically captioning a lecture presentation) and illustrate (synthesizing visual figures to accompany spoken explanations) educational content. We provide manual annotations to help implement these two research tasks and evaluate state-of-the-art models on them. Comparing baselines and human student performances, we find that current models struggle in (1) weak crossmodal alignment between slides and spoken text, (2) learning novel visual mediums, (3) technical language, and (4) long-range sequences. Towards addressing this issue, we also introduce PolyViLT, a multimodal transformer trained with a multi-instance learning loss that is more effective than current approaches. We conclude by shedding light on the challenges and opportunities in multimodal understanding of educational presentations.
summary: Lecture slide presentations, a sequence of pages that contain text and figures accompanied by speech, are constructed and presented carefully in order to optimally transfer knowledge to students. Previous studies in multimedia and psychology attribute the effectiveness of lecture presentations to their multimodal nature. As a step toward developing AI to aid in student learning as intelligent teacher assistants, we introduce the Multimodal Lecture Presentations dataset as a large-scale benchmark testing the capabilities of machine learning models in multimodal understanding of educational content. Our dataset contains aligned slides and spoken language, for 180+ hours of video and 9000+ slides, with 10 lecturers from various subjects (e.g., computer science, dentistry, biology). We introduce two research tasks which are designed as stepping stones towards AI agents that can explain (automatically captioning a lecture presentation) and illustrate (synthesizing visual figures to accompany spoken explanations) educational content. We provide manual annotations to help implement these two research tasks and evaluate state-of-the-art models on them. Comparing baselines and human student performances, we find that current models struggle in (1) weak crossmodal alignment between slides and spoken text, (2) learning novel visual mediums, (3) technical language, and (4) long-range sequences. Towards addressing this issue, we also introduce PolyViLT, a multimodal transformer trained with a multi-instance learning loss that is more effective than current approaches. We conclude by shedding light on the challenges and opportunities in multimodal understanding of educational presentations.
abstract_zh: 讲座幻灯片演示是一系列包含文本和图形并伴有演讲的页面，经过精心构建和呈现，以便以最佳方式向学生传授知识。先前的多媒体和心理学研究将讲座演讲的有效性归因于其多模态性质。作为开发人工智能以帮助学生作为智能教师助理学习的一步，我们引入了多模态讲座演示数据集作为大规模基准测试机器学习模型在多模态理解教育内容方面的能力。我们的数据集包含对齐的幻灯片和口语，包括 180 多个小时的视频和 9000 多张幻灯片，由来自不同学科（例如计算机科学、牙科、生物学）的 10 名讲师组成。我们介绍了两项研究任务，这些任务被设计为人工智能代理的垫脚石，可以解释（自动为讲座演示添加字幕）和说明（合成视觉图形以伴随口头解释）教育内容。我们提供手动注释来帮助实现这两项研究任务并评估它们的最先进模型。比较基线和人类学生的表现，我们发现当前模型在以下方面遇到困难：（1）幻灯片和口语文本之间的跨模式对齐较弱，（2）学习新颖的视觉媒体，（3）技术语言，以及（4）长距离序列。为了解决这个问题，我们还引入了 PolyViLT，这是一种经过多实例学习损失训练的多模态变压器，比当前的方法更有效。最后，我们阐明了教育演示的多模式理解所面临的挑战和机遇。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为Multimodal Lecture Presentations的大型数据集，用于评估机器学习模型在教育内容多模态理解方面的能力，并设计了两个研究任务（自动讲解生成和视觉合成）以推动智能教学助手的发展。
AI.motivation: 讲座幻灯片的多模态特性（文本、图像与语音结合）对知识传递至关重要，但现有模型在跨模态对齐、技术语言理解等任务上表现不足，需构建基准数据集推动相关研究。
AI.method: 构建包含180+小时视频和9000+幻灯片的数据集，涵盖10位不同学科讲师的对齐语音与幻灯片；设计自动讲解生成和视觉合成两个任务，并引入多实例学习的PolyViLT模型进行对比实验。
AI.result: 现有模型在跨模态对齐、新视觉媒介理解、技术术语处理及长序列建模上表现欠佳，而PolyViLT在基准测试中优于现有方法，但与人类学生表现仍存在差距。
AI.conclusion: 教育多模态内容理解面临跨模态对齐、长序列建模等挑战，但多实例学习框架（如PolyViLT）为智能教学助手的发展提供了新方向。

authors_text: Menghao Li,Wenquan Feng,Shuchang Lyu,Lijiang Chen,Qi Zhao
title: Look in Different Views: Multi-Scheme Regression Guided Cell Instance Segmentation
title_zh: 从不同的角度看：多方案回归引导的单元格实例分割
abstract: Cell instance segmentation is a new and challenging task aiming at joint detection and segmentation of every cell in an image. Recently, many instance segmentation methods have applied in this task. Despite their great success, there still exists two main weaknesses caused by uncertainty of localizing cell center points. First, densely packed cells can easily be recognized into one cell. Second, elongated cell can easily be recognized into two cells. To overcome these two weaknesses, we propose a novel cell instance segmentation network based on multi-scheme regression guidance. With multi-scheme regression guidance, the network has the ability to look each cell in different views. Specifically, we first propose a gaussian guidance attention mechanism to use gaussian labels for guiding the network's attention. We then propose a point-regression module for assisting the regression of cell center. Finally, we utilize the output of the above two modules to further guide the instance segmentation. With multi-scheme regression guidance, we can take full advantage of the characteristics of different regions, especially the central region of the cell. We conduct extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, DSB2018, CA2.5 and SCIS. The encouraging results show that our network achieves SOTA (state-of-the-art) performance. On the DSB2018 and CA2.5, our network surpasses previous methods by 1.2% (AP50). Particularly on SCIS dataset, our network performs stronger by large margin (3.0% higher AP50). Visualization and analysis further prove that our proposed method is interpretable.
summary: Cell instance segmentation is a new and challenging task aiming at joint detection and segmentation of every cell in an image. Recently, many instance segmentation methods have applied in this task. Despite their great success, there still exists two main weaknesses caused by uncertainty of localizing cell center points. First, densely packed cells can easily be recognized into one cell. Second, elongated cell can easily be recognized into two cells. To overcome these two weaknesses, we propose a novel cell instance segmentation network based on multi-scheme regression guidance. With multi-scheme regression guidance, the network has the ability to look each cell in different views. Specifically, we first propose a gaussian guidance attention mechanism to use gaussian labels for guiding the network's attention. We then propose a point-regression module for assisting the regression of cell center. Finally, we utilize the output of the above two modules to further guide the instance segmentation. With multi-scheme regression guidance, we can take full advantage of the characteristics of different regions, especially the central region of the cell. We conduct extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, DSB2018, CA2.5 and SCIS. The encouraging results show that our network achieves SOTA (state-of-the-art) performance. On the DSB2018 and CA2.5, our network surpasses previous methods by 1.2% (AP50). Particularly on SCIS dataset, our network performs stronger by large margin (3.0% higher AP50). Visualization and analysis further prove that our proposed method is interpretable.
abstract_zh: 细胞实例分割是一项新的且具有挑战性的任务，旨在联合检测和分割图像中的每个细胞。最近，许多实例分割方法已应用于此任务。尽管它们取得了巨大的成功，但由于定位单元中心点的不确定性，仍然存在两个主要弱点。首先，密集的细胞很容易被识别为一个细胞。其次，拉长的细胞很容易被识别为两个细胞。为了克服这两个弱点，我们提出了一种基于多方案回归指导的新型细胞实例分割网络。通过多方案回归指导，网络能够以不同的视图查看每个单元格。具体来说，我们首先提出一种高斯引导注意力机制，使用高斯标签来引导网络的注意力。然后，我们提出了一个点回归模块来辅助细胞中心的回归。最后，我们利用上述两个模块的输出来进一步指导实例分割。通过多方案回归指导，我们可以充分利用不同区域的特征，特别是细胞的中心区域。我们对基准数据集 DSB2018、CA2.5 和 SCIS 进行了广泛的实验。令人鼓舞的结果表明我们的网络实现了 SOTA（最先进的）性能。在 DSB2018 和 CA2.5 上，我们的网络超过了之前的方法 1.2% (AP50)。特别是在 SCIS 数据集上，我们的网络表现大幅增强（AP50 高出 3.0%）。可视化和分析进一步证明我们提出的方法是可解释的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多方案回归引导的细胞实例分割网络，解决密集和细长细胞分割中的误判问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法因细胞中心定位不确定性，导致密集细胞被误分为一个或细长细胞被误分为两个，亟需改进分割精度。
AI.method: 设计高斯引导注意力机制利用高斯标签引导网络关注区域，并引入点回归模块辅助细胞中心定位，结合多方案回归指导实例分割。
AI.result: 在DSB2018、CA2.5和SCIS数据集上达到SOTA性能，AP50分别提升1.2%和3.0%，可视化验证了方法可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 多方案回归引导机制有效利用细胞区域特征，显著提升分割精度，验证了方法在复杂场景下的优越性。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于多方案回归引导的细胞实例分割网络，解决密集和细长细胞分割中的误判问题。
motivation: 现有方法因细胞中心定位不确定性，导致密集细胞被误分为一个或细长细胞被误分为两个，亟需改进分割精度。
method: 设计高斯引导注意力机制利用高斯标签引导网络关注区域，并引入点回归模块辅助细胞中心定位，结合多方案回归指导实例分割。
result: 在DSB2018、CA2.5和SCIS数据集上达到SOTA性能，AP50分别提升1.2%和3.0%，可视化验证了方法可解释性。

search_text: Look in Different Views: Multi-Scheme Regression Guided Cell Instance Segmentation 从不同的角度看：多方案回归引导的单元格实例分割 Cell instance segmentation is a new and challenging task aiming at joint detection and segmentation of every cell in an image. Recently, many instance segmentation methods have applied in this task. Despite their great success, there still exists two main weaknesses caused by uncertainty of localizing cell center points. First, densely packed cells can easily be recognized into one cell. Second, elongated cell can easily be recognized into two cells. To overcome these two weaknesses, we propose a novel cell instance segmentation network based on multi-scheme regression guidance. With multi-scheme regression guidance, the network has the ability to look each cell in different views. Specifically, we first propose a gaussian guidance attention mechanism to use gaussian labels for guiding the network's attention. We then propose a point-regression module for assisting the regression of cell center. Finally, we utilize the output of the above two modules to further guide the instance segmentation. With multi-scheme regression guidance, we can take full advantage of the characteristics of different regions, especially the central region of the cell. We conduct extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, DSB2018, CA2.5 and SCIS. The encouraging results show that our network achieves SOTA (state-of-the-art) performance. On the DSB2018 and CA2.5, our network surpasses previous methods by 1.2% (AP50). Particularly on SCIS dataset, our network performs stronger by large margin (3.0% higher AP50). Visualization and analysis further prove that our proposed method is interpretable. 细胞实例分割是一项新的且具有挑战性的任务，旨在联合检测和分割图像中的每个细胞。最近，许多实例分割方法已应用于此任务。尽管它们取得了巨大的成功，但由于定位单元中心点的不确定性，仍然存在两个主要弱点。首先，密集的细胞很容易被识别为一个细胞。其次，拉长的细胞很容易被识别为两个细胞。为了克服这两个弱点，我们提出了一种基于多方案回归指导的新型细胞实例分割网络。通过多方案回归指导，网络能够以不同的视图查看每个单元格。具体来说，我们首先提出一种高斯引导注意力机制，使用高斯标签来引导网络的注意力。然后，我们提出了一个点回归模块来辅助细胞中心的回归。最后，我们利用上述两个模块的输出来进一步指导实例分割。通过多方案回归指导，我们可以充分利用不同区域的特征，特别是细胞的中心区域。我们对基准数据集 DSB2018、CA2.5 和 SCIS 进行了广泛的实验。令人鼓舞的结果表明我们的网络实现了 SOTA（最先进的）性能。在 DSB2018 和 CA2.5 上，我们的网络超过了之前的方法 1.2% (AP50)。特别是在 SCIS 数据集上，我们的网络表现大幅增强（AP50 高出 3.0%）。可视化和分析进一步证明我们提出的方法是可解释的。 本文提出一种基于多方案回归引导的细胞实例分割网络，解决密集和细长细胞分割中的误判问题。 Menghao Li,Wenquan Feng,Shuchang Lyu,Lijiang Chen,Qi Zhao cs.CV

authors_text: Debapriya Roy,Sanchayan Santra,Diganta Mukherjee,Bhabatosh Chanda
title: Significance of Skeleton-based Features in Virtual Try-On
title_zh: 基于骨架的特征在虚拟试穿中的意义
abstract: The idea of \textit{Virtual Try-ON} (VTON) benefits e-retailing by giving an user the convenience of trying a clothing at the comfort of their home. In general, most of the existing VTON methods produce inconsistent results when a person posing with his arms folded i.e., bent or crossed, wants to try an outfit. The problem becomes severe in the case of long-sleeved outfits. As then, for crossed arm postures, overlap among different clothing parts might happen. The existing approaches, especially the warping-based methods employing \textit{Thin Plate Spline (TPS)} transform can not tackle such cases. To this end, we attempt a solution approach where the clothing from the source person is segmented into semantically meaningful parts and each part is warped independently to the shape of the person. To address the bending issue, we employ hand-crafted geometric features consistent with human body geometry for warping the source outfit. In addition, we propose two learning-based modules: a synthesizer network and a mask prediction network. All these together attempt to produce a photo-realistic, pose-robust VTON solution without requiring any paired training data. Comparison with some of the benchmark methods clearly establishes the effectiveness of the approach.
summary: The idea of \textit{Virtual Try-ON} (VTON) benefits e-retailing by giving an user the convenience of trying a clothing at the comfort of their home. In general, most of the existing VTON methods produce inconsistent results when a person posing with his arms folded i.e., bent or crossed, wants to try an outfit. The problem becomes severe in the case of long-sleeved outfits. As then, for crossed arm postures, overlap among different clothing parts might happen. The existing approaches, especially the warping-based methods employing \textit{Thin Plate Spline (TPS)} transform can not tackle such cases. To this end, we attempt a solution approach where the clothing from the source person is segmented into semantically meaningful parts and each part is warped independently to the shape of the person. To address the bending issue, we employ hand-crafted geometric features consistent with human body geometry for warping the source outfit. In addition, we propose two learning-based modules: a synthesizer network and a mask prediction network. All these together attempt to produce a photo-realistic, pose-robust VTON solution without requiring any paired training data. Comparison with some of the benchmark methods clearly establishes the effectiveness of the approach.
abstract_zh: \textit{虚拟试穿} (VTON) 的理念让用户可以在舒适的家中方便地试穿衣服，从而有利于电子零售。一般来说，当一个人双臂交叉（即弯曲或交叉）想要尝试一套服装时，大多数现有的 VTON 方法都会产生不一致的结果。对于长袖服装，这个问题变得更加严重。这样，对于交叉手臂的姿势，不同衣服部分之间可能会发生重叠。现有的方法，特别是采用 \textit{Thin Plate Spline (TPS)} 变换的基于变形的方法无法解决此类情况。为此，我们尝试一种解决方案，将源人物的服装分割成语义上有意义的部分，并且每个部分都独立地扭曲成人物的形状。为了解决弯曲问题，我们采用与人体几何形状一致的手工几何特征来扭曲源服装。此外，我们提出了两个基于学习的模块：合成器网络和掩模预测网络。所有这些都试图产生一个逼真的、姿势稳健的 VTON 解决方案，而不需要任何配对的训练数据。与一些基准方法的比较清楚地证明了该方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于骨架特征的虚拟试穿方法，以解决现有方法在处理交叉手臂姿势时的不一致性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有虚拟试穿方法在用户手臂交叉时（尤其是长袖服装）容易产生衣物重叠，导致结果不一致，且基于TPS变形的方法无法有效处理此类情况。
AI.method: 将源人物服装分割为语义部分并独立变形，结合人体几何特征设计手工艺特征，同时引入合成网络和掩码预测网络，无需配对训练数据。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在基准测试中优于现有方法，能够生成逼真且姿态鲁棒的虚拟试穿结果。
AI.conclusion: 通过骨架特征与学习模块的结合，该方法有效解决了复杂姿态下的衣物变形问题，为无配对数据的虚拟试穿提供了新方案。
