authors_text: Jong-Wouk Kim,Yang-Sae Moon,Mi-Jung Choi
title: An Efficient Multi-Step Framework for Malware Packing Identification
title_zh: 恶意软件加壳识别的高效多步骤框架
abstract: Malware developers use combinations of techniques such as compression, encryption, and obfuscation to bypass anti-virus software. Malware with anti-analysis technologies can bypass AI-based anti-virus software and malware analysis tools. Therefore, classifying pack files is one of the big challenges. Problems arise if the malware classifiers learn packers' features, not those of malware. Training the models with unintended erroneous data turn into poisoning attacks, adversarial attacks, and evasion attacks. Therefore, researchers should consider packing to build appropriate malware classifier models. In this paper, we propose a multi-step framework for classifying and identifying packed samples which consists of pseudo-optimal feature selection, machine learning-based classifiers, and packer identification steps. In the first step, we use the CART algorithm and the permutation importance to preselect important 20 features. In the second step, each model learns 20 preselected features for classifying the packed files with the highest performance. As a result, the XGBoost, which learned the features preselected by XGBoost with the permutation importance, showed the highest performance of any other experiment scenarios with an accuracy of 99.67%, an F1-Score of 99.46%, and an area under the curve (AUC) of 99.98%. In the third step, we propose a new approach that can identify packers only for samples classified as Well-Known Packed.
summary: Malware developers use combinations of techniques such as compression, encryption, and obfuscation to bypass anti-virus software. Malware with anti-analysis technologies can bypass AI-based anti-virus software and malware analysis tools. Therefore, classifying pack files is one of the big challenges. Problems arise if the malware classifiers learn packers' features, not those of malware. Training the models with unintended erroneous data turn into poisoning attacks, adversarial attacks, and evasion attacks. Therefore, researchers should consider packing to build appropriate malware classifier models. In this paper, we propose a multi-step framework for classifying and identifying packed samples which consists of pseudo-optimal feature selection, machine learning-based classifiers, and packer identification steps. In the first step, we use the CART algorithm and the permutation importance to preselect important 20 features. In the second step, each model learns 20 preselected features for classifying the packed files with the highest performance. As a result, the XGBoost, which learned the features preselected by XGBoost with the permutation importance, showed the highest performance of any other experiment scenarios with an accuracy of 99.67%, an F1-Score of 99.46%, and an area under the curve (AUC) of 99.98%. In the third step, we propose a new approach that can identify packers only for samples classified as Well-Known Packed.
abstract_zh: 恶意软件开发人员使用压缩、加密和混淆等技术组合来绕过防病毒软件。具有反分析技术的恶意软件可以绕过基于人工智能的防病毒软件和恶意软件分析工具。因此，对包文件进行分类是一大挑战。如果恶意软件分类器了解加壳程序的特征，而不是恶意软件的特征，就会出现问题。使用无意的错误数据训练模型会导致中毒攻击、对抗性攻击和逃避攻击。因此，研究人员应该考虑打包构建合适的恶意软件分类器模型。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于分类和识别打包样本的多步骤框架，其中包括伪最优特征选择、基于机器学习的分类器和打包器识别步骤。第一步，我们使用CART算法和排列重要性来预选重要的20个特征。第二步，每个模型学习 20 个预选特征，用于以最高性能对打包文件进行分类。结果，学习了 XGBoost 预先选择的具有排列重要性的特征的 XGBoost 表现出了所有其他实验场景中最高的性能，准确率为 99.67%，F1 得分为 99.46%，曲线下面积 (AUC) 为 99.98%。在第三步中，我们提出了一种新方法，可以仅识别分类为知名加壳样品的加壳者。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多步骤框架，用于高效分类和识别打包的恶意软件样本。
AI.motivation: 恶意软件开发者通过压缩、加密等技术逃避杀毒软件，现有分类器可能因学习打包器特征而非恶意软件特征导致误判和攻击。
AI.method: 框架分三步：使用CART算法和排列重要性预选20个关键特征；基于机器学习分类器（如XGBoost）进行分类；针对已知打包样本提出新打包识别方法。
AI.result: XGBoost模型在预选特征下达到99.67%准确率、99.46% F1分数和99.98% AUC，优于其他实验场景。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过特征优化和分步识别有效提升恶意软件分类性能，为应对打包攻击提供新思路。

In the first step, we use the CART algorithm and the permutation importance to preselect important 20 features. In the second step, each model learns 20 preselected features for classifying the packed files with the highest performance. As a result, the XGBoost, which learned the features preselected by XGBoost with the permutation importance, showed the highest performance of any other experiment scenarios with an accuracy of 99.67%, an F1-Score of 99.46%, and an area under the curve (AUC) of 99.98%. In the third step, we propose a new approach that can identify packers only for samples classified as Well-Known Packed. 恶意软件开发人员使用压缩、加密和混淆等技术组合来绕过防病毒软件。具有反分析技术的恶意软件可以绕过基于人工智能的防病毒软件和恶意软件分析工具。因此，对包文件进行分类是一大挑战。如果恶意软件分类器了解加壳程序的特征，而不是恶意软件的特征，就会出现问题。使用无意的错误数据训练模型会导致中毒攻击、对抗性攻击和逃避攻击。因此，研究人员应该考虑打包构建合适的恶意软件分类器模型。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于分类和识别打包样本的多步骤框架，其中包括伪最优特征选择、基于机器学习的分类器和打包器识别步骤。第一步，我们使用CART算法和排列重要性来预选重要的20个特征。第二步，每个模型学习 20 个预选特征，用于以最高性能对打包文件进行分类。结果，学习了 XGBoost 预先选择的具有排列重要性的特征的 XGBoost 表现出了所有其他实验场景中最高的性能，准确率为 99.67%，F1 得分为 99.46%，曲线下面积 (AUC) 为 99.98%。在第三步中，我们提出了一种新方法，可以仅识别分类为知名加壳样品的加壳者。 本文提出一种多步骤框架，用于高效分类和识别打包的恶意软件样本。 Jong-Wouk Kim,Yang-Sae Moon,Mi-Jung Choi cs.CR,cs.AI

authors_text: Guangxuan Xu,Paulina Toro Isaza,Moshi Li,Akintoye Oloko,Bingsheng Yao,Cassia Sanctos,Aminat Adebiyi,Yufang Hou,Nanyun Peng,Dakuo Wang
title: NECE: Narrative Event Chain Extraction Toolkit
title_zh: NECE：叙事事件链提取工具包
abstract: To understand a narrative, it is essential to comprehend the temporal event flows, especially those associated with main characters; however, this can be challenging with lengthy and unstructured narrative texts. To address this, we introduce NECE, an open-access, document-level toolkit that automatically extracts and aligns narrative events in the temporal order of their occurrence. Through extensive evaluations, we show the high quality of the NECE toolkit and demonstrates its downstream application in analyzing narrative bias regarding gender. We also openly discuss the shortcomings of the current approach, and potential of leveraging generative models in future works. Lastly the NECE toolkit includes both a Python library and a user-friendly web interface, which offer equal access to professionals and layman audience alike, to visualize event chain, obtain narrative flows, or study narrative bias.
summary: To understand a narrative, it is essential to comprehend the temporal event flows, especially those associated with main characters; however, this can be challenging with lengthy and unstructured narrative texts. To address this, we introduce NECE, an open-access, document-level toolkit that automatically extracts and aligns narrative events in the temporal order of their occurrence. Through extensive evaluations, we show the high quality of the NECE toolkit and demonstrates its downstream application in analyzing narrative bias regarding gender. We also openly discuss the shortcomings of the current approach, and potential of leveraging generative models in future works. Lastly the NECE toolkit includes both a Python library and a user-friendly web interface, which offer equal access to professionals and layman audience alike, to visualize event chain, obtain narrative flows, or study narrative bias.
abstract_zh: 要理解叙事，必须理解时间事件流，尤其是与主要人物相关的事件流；然而，对于冗长且非结构化的叙述文本来说，这可能具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 NECE，这是一个开放访问的文档级工具包，可以按照事件发生的时间顺序自动提取和对齐叙述事件。通过广泛的评估，我们展示了 NECE 工具包的高质量，并展示了其在分析性别叙事偏见方面的下游应用。我们还公开讨论当前方法的缺点以及在未来工作中利用生成模型的潜力。最后，NECE 工具包包括一个 Python 库和一个用户友好的 Web 界面，为专业人士和外行观众提供平等的访问权限，以可视化事件链、获取叙述流或研究叙述偏差。

AI.tldr: 开发NECE工具包，用于从叙事文本中提取和对齐时间顺序的事件链
AI.motivation: 长篇和非结构化叙事文本中提取时间事件流存在挑战，需自动化工具辅助分析
AI.method: 基于文档级处理，结合Python库和网页界面，自动提取并按时间顺序对齐叙事事件
AI.result: 评估显示NECE工具包具有高精度，成功应用于分析性别叙事偏见，验证其有效性
AI.conclusion: NECE工具包有效支持叙事分析，但需进一步优化；未来结合生成模型可能提升性能，且工具具备广泛应用潜力

authors_text: Faranak Tohidi,Manoranjan Paul,Anwaar Ulhaq
title: Efficient dynamic point cloud coding using Slice-Wise Segmentation
title_zh: 使用切片分割进行高效的动态点云编码
abstract: With the fast growth of immersive video sequences, achieving seamless and high-quality compressed 3D content is even more critical. MPEG recently developed a video-based point cloud compression (V-PCC) standard for dynamic point cloud coding. However, reconstructed point clouds using V-PCC suffer from different artifacts, including losing data during pre-processing before applying existing video coding techniques, e.g., High-Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC). Patch generations and self-occluded points in the 3D to the 2D projection are the main reasons for missing data using V-PCC. This paper proposes a new method that introduces overlapping slicing as an alternative to patch generation to decrease the number of patches generated and the amount of data lost. In the proposed method, the entire point cloud has been cross-sectioned into variable-sized slices based on the number of self-occluded points so that data loss can be minimized in the patch generation process and projection. For this, a variable number of layers are considered, partially overlapped to retain the self-occluded points. The proposed method's added advantage is to reduce the bits requirement and to encode geometric data using the slicing base position. The experimental results show that the proposed method is much more flexible than the standard V-PCC method, improves the rate-distortion performance, and decreases the data loss significantly compared to the standard V-PCC method.
summary: With the fast growth of immersive video sequences, achieving seamless and high-quality compressed 3D content is even more critical. MPEG recently developed a video-based point cloud compression (V-PCC) standard for dynamic point cloud coding. However, reconstructed point clouds using V-PCC suffer from different artifacts, including losing data during pre-processing before applying existing video coding techniques, e.g., High-Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC). Patch generations and self-occluded points in the 3D to the 2D projection are the main reasons for missing data using V-PCC. This paper proposes a new method that introduces overlapping slicing as an alternative to patch generation to decrease the number of patches generated and the amount of data lost. In the proposed method, the entire point cloud has been cross-sectioned into variable-sized slices based on the number of self-occluded points so that data loss can be minimized in the patch generation process and projection. For this, a variable number of layers are considered, partially overlapped to retain the self-occluded points. The proposed method's added advantage is to reduce the bits requirement and to encode geometric data using the slicing base position. The experimental results show that the proposed method is much more flexible than the standard V-PCC method, improves the rate-distortion performance, and decreases the data loss significantly compared to the standard V-PCC method.
abstract_zh: 随着沉浸式视频序列的快速增长，实现无缝且高质量的压缩 3D 内容变得更加重要。 MPEG 最近开发了一种用于动态点云编码的基于视频的点云压缩 (V-PCC) 标准。然而，使用 V-PCC 重建的点云会遭受不同的伪像，包括在应用现有视频编码技术（例如高效视频编码 (HEVC)）之前的预处理过程中丢失数据。 3D 到 2D 投影中的面片生成和自遮挡点是使用 V-PCC 丢失数据的主要原因。本文提出了一种新方法，引入重叠切片作为补丁生成的替代方法，以减少生成的补丁数量和丢失的数据量。在所提出的方法中，根据自遮挡点的数量将整个点云截面为可变大小的切片，以便在补丁生成过程和投影过程中最大限度地减少数据丢失。为此，考虑了可变数量的层，部分重叠以保留自遮挡点。所提出的方法的附加优点是减少比特需求并使用切片基位置对几何数据进行编码。实验结果表明，该方法比标准V-PCC方法更加灵活，提高了率失真性能，并且与标准V-PCC方法相比显着减少了数据丢失。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于分片分割的高效动态点云编码方法，通过减少数据丢失和优化压缩性能来改进现有的V-PCC标准。
AI.motivation: 现有V-PCC方法在预处理阶段因补丁生成和自遮挡点问题导致数据丢失，影响点云重建质量。
AI.method: 采用重叠分片技术替代补丁生成，根据自遮挡点数量将点云分割为可变尺寸的分片，并通过部分重叠层保留自遮挡点以降低数据丢失。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在率失真性能、数据丢失减少方面显著优于标准V-PCC，且具有更高的灵活性。
AI.conclusion: 分片分割方法有效提升了动态点云压缩的效率和质量，为V-PCC标准提供了改进方向。

authors_text: Ji Xu,Gang Ren,Yao Xiao,Shaobo Li,Guoyin Wang
title: Semi-supervised Learning with Deterministic Labeling and Large Margin Projection
title_zh: 具有确定性标签和大裕度投影的半监督学习
comment: 12 pages, ready to submit to a journal
comments: 12 pages, ready to submit to a journal
abstract: The centrality and diversity of the labeled data are very influential to the performance of semi-supervised learning (SSL), but most SSL models select the labeled data randomly. This study first construct a leading forest that forms a partially ordered topological space in an unsupervised way, and select a group of most representative samples to label with one shot (differs from active learning essentially) using property of homeomorphism. Then a kernelized large margin metric is efficiently learned for the selected data to classify the remaining unlabeled sample. Optimal leading forest (OLF) has been observed to have the advantage of revealing the difference evolution along a path within a subtree. Therefore, we formulate an optimization problem based on OLF to select the samples. Also with OLF, the multiple local metrics learning is facilitated to address multi-modal and mix-modal problem in SSL, especially when the number of class is large. Attribute to this novel design, stableness and accuracy of the performance is significantly improved when compared with the state-of-the-art graph SSL methods. The extensive experimental studies have shown that the proposed method achieved encouraging accuracy and efficiency. Code has been made available at https://github.com/alanxuji/DeLaLA.
summary: The centrality and diversity of the labeled data are very influential to the performance of semi-supervised learning (SSL), but most SSL models select the labeled data randomly. This study first construct a leading forest that forms a partially ordered topological space in an unsupervised way, and select a group of most representative samples to label with one shot (differs from active learning essentially) using property of homeomorphism. Then a kernelized large margin metric is efficiently learned for the selected data to classify the remaining unlabeled sample. Optimal leading forest (OLF) has been observed to have the advantage of revealing the difference evolution along a path within a subtree. Therefore, we formulate an optimization problem based on OLF to select the samples. Also with OLF, the multiple local metrics learning is facilitated to address multi-modal and mix-modal problem in SSL, especially when the number of class is large. Attribute to this novel design, stableness and accuracy of the performance is significantly improved when compared with the state-of-the-art graph SSL methods. The extensive experimental studies have shown that the proposed method achieved encouraging accuracy and efficiency. Code has been made available at https://github.com/alanxuji/DeLaLA.
abstract_zh: 标记数据的中心性和多样性对半监督学习（SSL）的性能影响很大，但大多数 SSL 模型随机选择标记数据。本研究首先以无监督的方式构建一个形成偏序拓扑空间的引导森林，并利用同胚的性质选择一组最具代表性的样本进行一次性标记（本质上不同于主动学习）。然后，针对所选数据有效地学习核化大裕度度量，以对剩余的未标记样本进行分类。据观察，最优引导森林 (OLF) 具有揭示子树内路径上的差异演化的优点。因此，我们制定了基于OLF的优化问题来选择样本。此外，通过 OLF，多个局部度量学习有助于解决 SSL 中的多模态和混合模态问题，特别是当类别数量很大时。由于这种新颖的设计，与最先进的图 SSL 方法相比，性能的稳定性和准确性得到了显着提高。广泛的实验研究表明，所提出的方法取得了令人鼓舞的准确性和效率。代码已在 https://github.com/alanxuji/DeLaLA 上提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督学习方法，通过构建最优领先森林（OLF）选择代表性样本进行确定性标记，并利用大边距度量分类剩余未标记样本，以提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有半监督学习方法通常随机选择标记数据，忽视了标记数据的中心性和多样性对性能的影响，导致效果受限。
AI.method: 首先通过无监督方式构建领先森林，形成局部有序拓扑空间；利用同胚性质选择最具代表性的样本进行单次标记；再基于OLF学习核化大边距度量以分类未标记样本，并通过多局部度量学习解决多模态问题。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在稳定性与准确性上显著优于现有图半监督学习方法，且在大规模类别场景下表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 通过结构化数据选择与大边距度量学习的结合，本文方法有效提升了半监督学习的性能，为复杂数据场景提供了新思路。

search_text: Semi-supervised Learning with Deterministic Labeling and Large Margin Projection 具有确定性标签和大裕度投影的半监督学习 The centrality and diversity of the labeled data are very influential to the performance of semi-supervised learning (SSL), but most SSL models select the labeled data randomly. This study first construct a leading forest that forms a partially ordered topological space in an unsupervised way, and select a group of most representative samples to label with one shot (differs from active learning essentially) using property of homeomorphism. Then a kernelized large margin metric is efficiently learned for the selected data to classify the remaining unlabeled sample. Optimal leading forest (OLF) has been observed to have the advantage of revealing the difference evolution along a path within a subtree. Therefore, we formulate an optimization problem based on OLF to select the samples. Also with OLF, the multiple local metrics learning is facilitated to address multi-modal and mix-modal problem in SSL, especially when the number of class is large. Attribute to this novel design, stableness and accuracy of the performance is significantly improved when compared with the state-of-the-art graph SSL methods. The extensive experimental studies have shown that the proposed method achieved encouraging accuracy and efficiency. Code has been made available at https://github.com/alanxuji/DeLaLA. 标记数据的中心性和多样性对半监督学习（SSL）的性能影响很大，但大多数 SSL 模型随机选择标记数据。本研究首先以无监督的方式构建一个形成偏序拓扑空间的引导森林，并利用同胚的性质选择一组最具代表性的样本进行一次性标记（本质上不同于主动学习）。然后，针对所选数据有效地学习核化大裕度度量，以对剩余的未标记样本进行分类。据观察，最优引导森林 (OLF) 具有揭示子树内路径上的差异演化的优点。因此，我们制定了基于OLF的优化问题来选择样本。此外，通过 OLF，多个局部度量学习有助于解决 SSL 中的多模态和混合模态问题，特别是当类别数量很大时。由于这种新颖的设计，与最先进的图 SSL 方法相比，性能的稳定性和准确性得到了显着提高。广泛的实验研究表明，所提出的方法取得了令人鼓舞的准确性和效率。代码已在 https://github.com/alanxuji/DeLaLA 上提供。 本文提出一种半监督学习方法，通过构建最优领先森林（OLF）选择代表性样本进行确定性标记，并利用大边距度量分类剩余未标记样本，以提升模型性能。 Ji Xu,Gang Ren,Yao Xiao,Shaobo Li,Guoyin Wang cs.AI
